events.name: AI Community Conference
events.description: Join us at the AI Community Conference in New York for 2 Days of networking, learning, & discussing all things artificial intelligence!  Exciting Announcement: Join Us for a Cutting-Edge AI Community Event in New York City!  Are you ready to dive into the thrilling world of Artificial Intelligence (AI)? Look no further! We're thrilled to invite you to a dynamic & engaging community event focused on AI, set to take place right in the heart of New York City.  Pre-Day Workshop: June 5th, 2025 Time: 10 a.m. EST Venue: Microsoft, Times Square    Main Event: June 6th, 2025 Time: 9 a.m. EST Venue: Microsoft, Times Square  Pre-Day Workshop: Hands-On AI TrainingKickstart your AI experience with an exclusive pre-day workshop designed for professionals, developers, & enthusiasts looking to gain hands-on experience with AI tools & techniques. Led by industry experts, this interactive session will cover:  AI Model Development: Learn how to build & fine-tune AI models. Practical AI Applications: Explore real-world case studies & implementation strategies. Live Demos & Q&A: Engage with experts, ask questions, & get practical insights. At this exclusive gathering, you'll also have the opportunity to explore the latest trends, breakthroughs, & applications in AI technology alongside fellow enthusiasts, experts, & industry leaders. Whether you're a seasoned professional or just beginning your journey in AI, there's something for everyone at this event. Here's a sneak peek of what's in store at the main event: Inspiring Keynote + Speaker Presentations - Gain insights from renowned AI experts as they share their vision for the future of AI & its impact across various industries.  Thought-Provoking Panel Discussions - Engage in discussions on AI ethics, diversity, & the societal implications of AI adoption. Your perspective matters, so join the conversation!  Sponsor Showcases - Discover groundbreaking AI startups & their innovative solutions shaping the future of technology.  Networking Opportunities - Connect with like-minded individuals, forge valuable connections, & expand your professional network within the vibrant AI community of New York City.  Fun & Entertainment - Enjoy live demonstrations, AI-powered activities, & interactive exhibits that promise to entertain & inspire attendees of all ages. Don't miss out on this incredible opportunity to immerse yourself in the fascinating world of AI & connect with fellow enthusiasts & experts. Please reserve your spot today & get ready to embark on an unforgettable AI journey with us! Stay tuned for more updates & surprises. We can't wait to welcome you to the AI event of the year in New York City!  Reach out if you would like to sponsor & participate in the conference.

events.name: Leaddev Meetup
events.description: Get ready for the next LeadDev New York Meetups!  Cant wait until the fall to get your LeadDev New York fix? No need to worry - quarterly LeadDev Meetups are back in New York! Join us for the next event on Monday, June 2nd & enjoy an evening of networking & talks on building effective engineering culture & growing leaders. Drinks & nibbles will be provided on the night, thanks to Datadog! Date: Monday, June 2nd, 2025 Time: 6:00 - 8:30 pm Location: New York Times Building, 620 8th Ave, New York, NY 10018 Spaces are limited - you must RSVP to attend. Attendees must also bring a government-issued ID with a name that matches their Meetup registration to enter the New York Times Building. We hope to see you there! Schedule: 6.00 pm - Arrival, networking & refreshments 6.20 pm - Welcome from host - James Courtois (Senior Director of Engineering Manager, Luminate) 6.30 pm - Strategic Approaches to Deploying LLMs in Your Organization - Katerina Zanos (Senior Machine Learning Engineer, Meta) 6.50 pm - Can AI build our AI strategy for us, too? - A Conversation on AI in Organizational Leadership Patty Delgado (VP Engineering, Vividly) & Meg Adams (Senior Director of Engineering, NYT Cooking) 7.10 pm - Break 7.15 pm - The Devops Engineer who never sleeps: Lessons Building AI Agents @ Datadog - Diamond Bishop (Director of Engineering/AI, Datadog) 7.35 pm - Closing remarks & networking through to close 8.30 pm - Close You can also join our Slack workspace to keep up to date with everything in our New York community. Join the channel #leaddev-nyc or #new-york. If you need further information or have any questions, please send an email to: hello@leaddev.com. Here is a friendly reminder of our Code of Conduct to help protect the safety of our community. Don't forget to share this with your colleagues who may be interested, & keep an eye out for upcoming meetups later this year! And a big thank you to our New York meetup partner Datadog!

events.name: Building Emotional Support Systems w/ AI
events.description: This isn't just another tech panel-join us for an intimate, women-led roundtable exploring how AI can support emotional wellbeing, digital balance, & addiction recovery in today's high-pressure, hyperconnected world. This gathering will break into small, thoughtfully curated groups to spark honest conversations, not lectures. Together with 50 mission-driven technologists, builders, & community leaders, we'll dive deep into designing a more compassionate digital future.  Core topics we'll explore: How AI can offer non-judgmental, always-on emotional support Rethinking digital wellness & social media through a gender-inclusive lens Exploring voice vs. text agents for crisis care Designing pro-user algorithms that empower attention-not exploit it Our hosts:EchoHer is a non-profit organization dedicated to empowering women & non-binary founders & funders by increasing their representation & success in entrepreneurship. We foster a supportive ecosystem to drive meaningful change & create impactful opportunities through monthly events, co-working opportunities, curated networking sessions, pitch nights, & insightful panel discussions. PowerYou is an AI-powered companion for emotional healing & wellbeing. Designed for people navigating addiction, burnout, or self-doubt, it offers daily support through guided reflection, personalized action plans, & a warm, human-like AI guide named Kris. We imagine a world where no one has to suffer alone in their hardest moments - because the support they need is right in their pocket. Already ready to listen. Always learning. Always there. This event is a part of #NYTechWeek-a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

events.name: NYC Hardware Meetup
events.description: NYC Hardware Builders Meetup  Round 2nTo everyone who came to the last meetupthank you! It was a great time, & I loved hearing what everyone had to share. Thats why were doing it again.nThis time, were adding lightning talksshort, sharp, & idea-packed. If youre working on something cool (or learned something the hard way), grab the mic & tell us about it.nnAbout the event:nThe NYC hardware scene is growing fastand it deserves a tight-knit, high-quality community. Thats why I started this meetup: to connect the people actually building real things. Whether you're into embedded systems, wearables, neurotech, robotics, or RF, this is where we meet, share, & push each other forward.nCome meet fellow engineers, inventors, & builders shaping the future of hardware. Whether you're debugging a prototype, bringing up a custom board, or just love the smell of solder in the morningthis is your scene.nBring your projects, your ideas, or just your curiosity. Bonus points if you bring something you built.

events.name: DeSciNYC - AI & Govt
events.description: AbstractNew York is considering some of the most ambitious AI regulations in the country. Assemblymember Alex Bores, who introduced several of the bills, will explain what's on the table & take questions in an open Q&A. ----- BioAlex Bores is a New York State Assembly Member for Manhattan's East Side, recognized for proactive AI legislation. A former data scientist at Palantir with a master's in computer science, he left due to ethical concerns over the company's ICE collaboration. Bores introduced several AI bills focused on consumer protections, data transparency, & digital content authenticity. Elected in 2022 as the first Democrat in the legislature with a computer science degree, he collaborates closely with tech companies to create effective AI policies. ----- Supported ByStadium ScienceFreedom ----- Stay Connected about Future EventsTwitter: @descinyc_Instagram: @descinyc PS: Apply to be a member of the DeSci NYC group chat: https://forms.gle/AmUtotmCgWgGxWDp6

events.name: Growth Mindset Founders Breakfast
events.description: This NY Tech Week, take a breath. A-Players is hosting an intimate morning gathering for Series A-stage founders & investors, featuring a guided mindfulness session led by Irka Ryabaya (ex-Goldman Sachs), a mental health coach & former founder herself.  You'll reflect on your journey, connect with peers through thoughtful dialogue, & enjoy a curated breakfast with others who understand the highs & lows of scaling a startup. With a network of 650+ founders & partnerships with top VCs, A-Players creates highly curated, private events designed to foster real connections & shared growth. We are proud to partner with Stifel Venture Bank & TriNet for this event. Stifel Financial (NYSE: SF) is a global investment bank, wealth management, & commercial banking institution with $400B+ in AUM. Stifel Bank offers venture & fund banking solutions, including 100% free banking for fund clients, fully insured liquid ICS accounts (up to $250MM FDIC coverage), capital call & management company lines of credit, & venture lending. Learn more at https://bankwithstifel.com/venture-banking/ Raising money from investors requires you to show you can run a business. You need a full-service HR solution tailored for growing technology companies. With decades of experience & roots in Silicon Valley, TriNet provides HR expertise to help you grow your company & stay compliant. By working with them, you can focus on what's important-raising capital, launching innovative solutions & scaling your team. This event is a part of #NYTechWeek-a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

events.name: Bedrock Agents Workshop
events.description: Join us for a hands-on introduction to AI Agents, where you will learn to optimize task automation using generative AI. This session will demonstrate how these agents leverage foundation models to interpret complex requests, integrate with your APIs & data, & execute multistep workflows seamlessly.  Key highlights:  Multi-agent Collaboration: Coordinate specialized agents under a supervisor to manage intricate processes. RAG Integration: Provide accurate, data-supported responses through retrieval-augmented generation. Configuration: Build & customize agents efficiently with straightforward instructions.   Discover practical, real-world applications & gain the skills to transform workflows with AI-powered solutions that boost efficiency & elevate user experiences. Together, let us pave the way for intelligent automation.  This event features an interactive workshopplease bring your laptop if you wish to participate in the hands-on segment. Are you prepared to advance your organizations AI capabilities? Mark your calendar for June 9, 2025! High level schedule: 2:45 - 3:00: Arrivals 3:00 - 3:20: Presentation 3:20 - 4:20: Workshop 4:20 - 4:30: Wrap up & Q&A 4:30 - 5:00: Networking Bio: Udaya Jaladi is a Solutions Architect at AWS specializing in FinTech ISV customers. With over 16 years of experience, he has extensive expertise in designing & implementing enterprise applications across Sales, Services, Financial, HR, & Payroll domains. In his current role, he partners with customers to provide strategic guidance on building well-architected, AI-powered solutions on AWS. Udaya has developed deep expertise in Generative AI & helps organizations leverage this transformative technology to drive business innovation & efficiency Important instructions  Event starts at 3:00 pm EST, but please allow at least 15 minutes for security to process registration Please ensure that your meetup profile has your full name. Both first & last name are required & we will not be able to register attendees with just abbreviations or incomplete names. There is an optional networking & Q&A event at the end of the meetup

events.name: Founder's Guide To Fractional
events.description: Sponsored by: iDelsoft What's a Fractional-and why are founders hiring them? Come for the food & drinks, stay for the insight. This interactive event gives you direct access to top Fractional execs across Product, Marketing, Finance, & more. If you're scaling a startup, this is the high-impact talent you didn't know you needed-until now. Whether you're curious, skeptical, or actively hiring part-time leaders, this event is your crash course in what fractional really means, when it makes sense, & how to make it work for your business. No pitch decks. No fluff. Just smart people & real talk.  Agenda 6:00-6:30pm - Arrival, Drinks & MingleEase in with music, light bites, & good energy.  6:30-6:45pm - Opening RemarksWe'll kick things off with a quick, founder-friendly download:  What a fractional leader is  Why this model is taking off in tech  How to make the most of the next two hours Think of it as your roadmap for navigating the night.  6:45-7:45pm - Rotating Stations: Meet the Fractionals Explore stations by function-Product, Marketing, Finance, People, Strategy, & more. Each station offers:  Real conversations with experienced fractional leaders - your chance to ask the spicy questions (like is fractional really productive?) & debunk the biggest myths  A prompt to guide the convo Move at your own pace or follow suggested rotations.  7:45-8:15pm - Group Reflection & Real TalkWe'll gather for a quick & candid debrief:  Founders: What did you learn or realize tonight?  Fractionals: What misconceptions do people still have?  Open mic for Q&A, insights, & follow-ups  8:15-8:30pm - Closing & Open MingleStick around to swap contacts, take selfies, & keep the conversation going. You never know who you'll meet or what spark will come from it. This event is a part of #NYTechWeek-a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

events.name: Hospitality's AI Moment - From Insights To Impact
events.description: AI is moving fastbut in hospitality, the stakes are timeless: service, timing, loyalty, & growth. This NYC Tech Week salon brings together hospitality leaders & AI operators for a real-world conversation on how customer intelligence is evolving. What does it mean to apply AI across restaurants & hotelswhere customers might be B2B or guests, but expectations are always high? With featured guests:Robin Chiang  Chief Growth Officer, OpenTableElise Tagatac  Chief Growth Officer, Mr & Mrs Smith Hotels Facilitated by: Deepak Shrivastava, CEO, Sunrise AI This is a curated salon for senior leaders across hospitality, GTM, & AIcapped at ~20 participants. The format is conversational & off-record. The goal is to make this a high-signal room of people who are actively building or leading in this space. This event is a part of #NYTechWeeka week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com. -- Robin is a seasoned executive in travel & dining technology, currently serving as Chief Growth Officer at OpenTable. He previously ran the international business at KAYAK. With a seasoned background in private equity, venture capital, investment banking & management consulting across Europe & Greater China, he brings a global, multi-disciplinary perspective to his leadership. Robin holds an MBA from the University of Chicago Booth School of Business, as well degrees in law & computer engineering from the University of Melbourne. He was honored as an Asia 21 Fellow by the Asia Society.  (edited)  -- Elise Tagatac is the Chief Growth Officer at Mr & Mrs Smith, an award-winning boutique & luxury hotel booking service specializing in the worlds most seductive stays, now part of the Hyatt family. A founding member of the companys US team, Elise has been instrumental in expanding its global footprint since 2010. She holds a critical global role, focused on driving business growth through hotel partnerships, reseller distribution, & strategic development opportunities. Serving as a key member of the Operating Board, Elise influences the company's strategic direction & helps shape its future.

events.name: FirstGen Founders Powering American Dynamism
events.description: Join FirstGen for FirstGen Founders Powering American Dynamism, a gathering that spotlights the immigrant & first-generation entrepreneurs building the next wave of critical American industries. From defense tech & manufacturing to public infrastructure, logistics, & energy-these founders are driving the innovation that keeps America competitive & resilient. This event brings together founders, investors, & operators who are not only redefining sectors historically dominated by legacy players but also proving that diverse, globally-minded leadership is essential to American progress & global leadership. Who Should Attend Immigrant & first-generation founders building in "American Dynamism" sectors Investors focused on defense, logistics, public sector tech, climate, & infrastructure Operators, policymakers, & ecosystem builders supporting this next frontier of innovation Panelists:  Andrew Stroup - CEO & Cofounder | Leverage AI Andrew Stroup is a Korean American adoptee & the founder of Leverage, an AI-powered supply chain purchase order automation & visibility platform. He is a serial technology entrepreneur, investor, & advisor with domain expertise in aerospace, software, supply chain, cybersecurity, robotics, & government. Leverage has raised $10m to date & select enterprise customers include Systems Control, Halo (Amphenol), & ITW. Previously, Andrew was the Director of Product & Technology for the White House Presidential Innovation Fellows, CEO of an identity & access management security software startup. He also served as a DoD civilian focused on the Biosurveillance White House Initiative, partnering with countries such as South Korea. His support to the Armed Forces also included oversight of medical countermeasures & Chemical, Biological, Radiological, & Nuclear materiel development as well as Joint Strike Fighter (F-35) integration efforts. Andrew earned dual degrees in Aerospace Engineering & Mechanical Engineering with a focus on Mathematics & Business Management from Oklahoma State University.  Sage Seckin Secilmis - President | WebSensing Sage Seckin Secilmis is the President of WebSensing, a single-chip network security solutions providing government agencies & approved partners to enable all-domain command & control through secure data transfers. Prior to WebSensing, Seckin's leadership experience has spanned industries & roles, including leading the successful exit of space tech startup Tethers Unlimited as the COO & GM; leading a large, 150-person, cross-disciplinary engineering team producing AED devices; & leading the development of Boeing's B737 BSI Mood-Lighting System which flies on every aircraft in production today & has over a dozen patents. Seckin has an MBA from C.W. Post, Long Island University, & a B.S. in Computer Engineering Technology from Rochester Institute of Technology.  Ann Xu - CEO & Co-founder | ElectroTempo Dr. Ann Xu is co-founder & CEO of ElectroTempo. ElectroTempo is a Software-as-a-Service company focused on energy infrastructure planning & intelligence for electric utilities, charging site hosts, & fleets. ElectroTempo has raised $5m to date & select enterprise customers include Prologis & Austin Energy. Dr. Xu spun out ElectroTempo from Texas A&M Transportation Institute (TTI), where she led the research & development of large-scale regional electric vehicle charging demand simulators. Prior to TTI, Dr. Xu was Senior Technical Advisor for Impact & Assessment at the U.S. Department of Energy's Advanced Research Projects Agency-Energy (ARPA-E). Dr. Xu received her Ph.D. in Transportation Systems Engineering from the Georgia Institute of Technology & her dual B.S. degrees in Environmental Science & Statistics from Peking University in China.  David Pietrocola - CEO & Co-founder | Cohesive Robotics David Pietrocola is Co-Founder & CEO at Cohesive Robotics, a leading developer of AI-powered turnkey, & pre-engineered robotic workcells for high-mix, high-part variability environments. He has led numerous teams & developed cutting-edge products in industry & national security. His work helps run automation & AI systems in manufacturing & logistics at world-class organizations such as Daimler, Tesla, Walgreens & the CIA. He's a lead inventor on multiple patents, graduated Phi Beta Kappa with a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from Trinity College, & earned a M.S. in Systems Engineering from the University of Pennsylvania. About the Hosts & Sponsors: FirstGen is an organization dedicated to expanding access to scalable entrepreneurship & venture capital for immigrant & first-generation (children of immigrants) entrepreneurs. Our goal is to help founders raise their first $1M in funding and/or reach their first $1M in revenue. We do so by supporting founders through capability-building, capital & customer support & community. Special thanks to our sponsors, EisnerAmper - EisnerAmper, one of the largest business consulting firms in the world, is comprised of EisnerAmper LLP, a licensed independent CPA firm that provides client attest services; & Eisner Advisory Group, an alternative practice structure that provides business advisory & non-attest services in accordance with all applicable laws, regulations, standards & codes of conduct. Clients are in all business sectors & leverage a complete menu of service offerings. Our Technology Practice professionals are a dedicated & agile team who serve the specialized needs of more than 2,100 public & private technology companies, as well as investors. Our combined entities include nearly 4,500 employees & 440 partners. For more information, please visit eisneramper.com, & explore our thought leadership resource library at www.eisneramper.com/knowledge-center.  HSBC Innovation Banking is a dedicated banking practice focused on the innovation economy. It brings HSBC's expansive product suite & international capabilities to serve high-growth innovative businesses from startups to scale-ups & beyond. HSBC Innovation Banking supports entrepreneurs & investors in tech, life sciences, & other fast-moving industries with an unmatched global network, deep sector expertise, & tailored financial products designed specifically to fuel growth in the innovation ecosystem, including a range of debt & credit facilities to suit all sizes of businesses. Backed by the strength & stability of HSBC, it combines a startup-friendly approach with world-class, scalable, banking infrastructure. Dentons - As a boutique practice within the world's largest law firm, Dentons' Global Venture Technology Group offers nimble, strategic counsel to leading emerging tech companies & venture investors. We bring together local market expertise, global reach & a Silicon Valley mindset-delivering the tailored support & insights needed to drive innovation & growth. By signing up, you are agreeing to join our email distribution lists. Thank you

events.description: Join Startup Grind on Friday, May 30th for Founder Drinks Roundtable. We're putting the "happy" back in "happy hour." Because networking events should be fun, especially on a Friday. We're putting the "happy" back in "happy hour" -- without the eye-rolls of formal presentations, high-stakes pitches, or waiting to get approved.  Because networking events should be fun, especially on a Friday. Join Startup Grind on Friday, May 30th at Shades of Green near Union Square for Founder Drinks Roundtable. We'll chat & laugh & maybe even solve for AGI (or at least be convinced that we have). Look for the Startup Grind table signs.  Whether you're an early-stage startup, a seasoned entrepreneur, or someone passionate about innovation, the Founder Drinks Roundtable provides a relaxed & inclusive environment to foster connections & celebrate the entrepreneurial spirit.

events.name: Founder & Funder Power Walk
events.description: We are kicking off NY Tech Week with a power walk! Connect with startup founders, funders, developers, & tech professionals while walking, networking, & getting fresh air-all in one power-packed hour! staaake Power Walk: Meet People Without Wasting Time A power walk is a themed social walk where you meet startup founders, funders, developers, & anyone involved in tech-all while getting your steps in. No awkward small talk, just a refreshing break from work & screens. Why a Power Walk is Better Than a Traditional Meetup  Meetups - Slow, awkward, & often a hit or miss. You might leave thinking, Was that really worth my time?  Power Walks - No wasted time. You get fresh air, 6,000 steps, & meaningful conversations-all in just one hour! Even if the group isn't great, you still got your walk in. Dress Code for the Walk to Stand Out  Looking for funding? Wear black. Investor? Wear white or a button-down shirt. Networking? Wear something colorful. Bonus: Join Our Private WhatsApp Group  People who join our walk will be in our exclusive NY Tech Week 2025 WhatsApp group! Free WhatsApp Channel here: https://whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb9wzfC6BIEg7UVtRq1Y staaake NY Tech Week 2025 Excel List Stand out from thousands of attendees by being on the staaake Excel list. This will set you apart & make you more visible. Have your name, link (LinkedIn or company), & what you're looking for-funding, investing, or networking-added to the list. This list will be linked to the over 20 events we are hosting for NY Tech Week on Partiful, Lu.ma, Eventbrite, & Meetup, & will also be emailed to everyone who signed up for those events! Get the staaake swag, support the movement - no matter where you are. NY Tech Week Special Order the I'm Her or I'm Him hat & get: 10% off Early access to the NY Tech Week WhatsApp group Get listed on our Google Sheet list  Use discount code at checkout: nytechweek https://gsonlinestore.com/collections/staaake/staaake  Worldwide shipping Stand Out & Get Exposure!  Be part of the staaake List, featured on all our events worldwide. Get noticed, make connections, & put yourself on the map!   Join the list: https://www.staaake.in/?staaake (Copy the full URL to avoid redirection to the homepage on some iPhones.)   Arrive Early!  We leave on time & won't be able to text latecomers. Please respect other people's time, too! Event Details  Meet at: In Front of Shake Shack Meatpacking District , 820 Washington St, New York, NY 10014 Lead Power Walker: https://www.staaake.in/?rahhh (Copy the full URL to avoid redirection to the homepage on some iPhones.) NY Tech Week 2024 opening power walk, we took over the high line over 200 people, we planning to double it this year! https://lu.ma/staaake Instagram: @staaake How to Become a Sponsor To feature your organization as a main sponsor across all 25 of our NY Tech Week events, we offer the following: Lu.ma: Your logo & a link to your website will be displayed in each event's description. Eventbrite & Meetup: Your organization's name & a link to your website will be included in each event's description. This sponsorship ensures consistent visibility & recognition throughout our event series. To view all our events, please visit: https://lu.ma/staaake?tag=nytechweek Location

events.name: AI In Recruiting - But At What Cost?
events.description: In this panel discussion, Brett Martin will lead a group of expert founders & HR executives from leading scaleups & Fortune 500 companies as they explore the tradeoffs of introducing AI into the recruiting process. events.name: Demos & Dim Sum
events.description: A Technical Show & Tell for AI Builders Technical AI builders only: Science fair meets dim sum during NY Tech Week. Share your foundation model experiments in a collegial setting where everyone brings working code to demo. Connect with peers actively building LLMs while enjoying a traditional dim sum experience. No pitches or business networking-just practitioners showing real technical work. Space limited to maintain our high technical standards & ensure meaningful conversations. Event Details Date: During NYC Tech WeekTime: 12:00 PM (Noon)Location: Chinatown NYC (RSVP for location)Format: Interactive show & tell over dim sum cart service.The Concept: Bring Your Build Every builder must bring their laptop with something to demo-whether it's a new feature, a technical challenge you're solving, or a breakthrough your team just achieved. As the dim sum carts circle, you'll share your work with peers in an informal, science-fair style format. What Kind of Projects Should I Bring? We welcome a variety of projects at different stages: Works in Progress: Technical challenges you're actively solving that could benefit from other builders' insightsRecent Breakthroughs: New features or capabilities your team has just implementedTechnical Experiments: Side projects or experiments that demonstrate innovative approachesBehind-the-Scenes Tools: Internal tools or infrastructure that power your productIntegration Challenges: Interesting solutions to integration problems between systems or APIsAI Model Implementations: Novel implementations or fine-tuning approaches you've developedThe ideal demo is something you can show in 3-5 minutes that sparks technical conversation. It doesn't need to be polished-authentic technical challenges often lead to the most valuable discussions. Who Should Attend Exclusively for builders who: Are hands-on with their product & technologyHave something tangible to demonstrate (no slideware)Value giving & receiving technical feedback from fellow buildersWhat to Expect Interactive showcase format-every builder brings a demo, something to tinker onCasual table rotation to see what others are buildingMonitors available at each table for easier demonstrationsAuthentic dim sum dining experience with traditional cart serviceWhy Join In a sea of networking events during Tech Week, this gathering stands apart by focusing on what builders are actually building. Connect with peers who understand both the technical & business challenges you face, & form relationships based on genuine interest in each other's innovations. Space is strictly limited to 30-35. Your ticket to entry? Your laptop & something to share. Bring your build, share your challenges, & leave with meaningful connections to fellow technical builders.

events.name: Influence & Advocacy For Engg Leaders
events.description: Join our Engineering Leadership NYC event, in collaboration with LinkedIn, focused on discussing the experiences of women engineering leaders. We're welcoming all engineering leaders to be part of the audience. This event will explore how engineering leaders can expand their impact by fostering relationships, advocating for themselves & others, & driving meaningful change across teams & organizations. The topic will dive deep into: 1. How to foster influence through relationships & trust, cultivating allies & making your voice heard with your team/organization. 2. Advocacy as a leadership tool, examining ways to champion both your own contributions & the success of others, creating space for diverse perspectives. 3. Overcoming challenges unique to underrepresented groups in tech, including navigating implicit biases, breaking through communication barriers, & leading effectively Agenda 6:00 PM - ReceptionCheck into the event, & enjoy food & drinks while networking with other engineering leaders. 6:30 PM - Lightning TalksEngage with two short 15 minute presentations on the event topic by: - Christine Choi - Senior Staff Data Scientist @ LinkedIn- Nitya Shekar - Executive Coach & Founder of Second Wind Group 7:25 PM - Panel Discussion + Live Q&ADeep dive into the topic of the event & direct your live questions to: - Christine Choi - Senior Staff Data Scientist @ LinkedIn- Nitya Shekar - Executive Coach & Founder of Second Wind Group- Yannie Yip - Engineering Manager @ Asana- Jin Zhang - Director of Engineering @ Meta Moderated by: - Siri Kostanyan - Co-organizer @ Engineering Leadership NYC MC'ed by: - Alegria Baquero - Engineering Manager @ Zocdoc 8:10 PM - NetworkingMake new connections & continue the discussion with fellow leaders until 8:30pm. About Engineering Leadership NYC Engineering Leadership NYC is a community for Engineering Managers, Directors, VPs, & Staff+ Engineers working in the software industry. The organization hosts in-person events focused on fostering connections, sharing experiences, & building professional relationships. Through curated topics, panel discussions, & networking opportunities, we provide a space for leaders to navigate the challenges of engineering management & leadership together. About LinkedIn Founded in 2003, LinkedIn connects the world's professionals to make them more productive & successful. With more than 1 billion members worldwide, including executives from every Fortune 500 company, LinkedIn is the world's largest professional network. The company has a diversified business model with revenue coming from Talent Solutions, Marketing Solutions, Sales Solutions & Premium Subscriptions products. Headquartered in Silicon Valley, LinkedIn has offices across the globe. This event is a part of #NYTechWeek-a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com. Attendee Confirmation Principles To ensure fairness, relevance of the event, & transparency in our RSVP approval process, we follow these principles when confirming event attendees: 1.  Relevance of BackgroundWe will prioritize applicants with an engineering leadership role & experience that align with the mission of our community and/or are relevant to the topic of the event. 2. Prior Event Attendee PriorityIf space is limited, existing community members who have attended our prior events will be prioritized over new applicants. 3. First-Come, First-ServedOtherwise, RSVPs will generally be confirmed in the order they are received. Thank you for your understanding!

events.name: Legal Leaders Meetup
events.description: We're bringing together a select group of legal leaders for an afternoon of thought-provoking conversation, peer connection, & rooftop views over lunch & refreshments.   We're delighted to be joined by:  Meredith Grauer, Head of Legal, Global Monetization, E-Commerce & Payments Privacy at TikTok  Gabrielle Kohlmeier, Global Head of Legal Innovation + Emerging Solutions at Verizon  Carly McKenna, Head of CS, Trust & Safety, EMEA at AirBnB   An innovative & inclusive leader, Meredith brings decades of experience building & leading global privacy & data protection programs across the professional services, media, ad tech, financial services, & fintech sectors.   Before joining TikTok, Meredith held senior roles at Marqeta, Nielsen, PwC, & Deutsche Bank, where she advised on regulatory compliance, privacy strategy, risk mitigation, & legal operations. Her unique perspective will shape a dynamic conversation on the future of legal leadership in a privacy-first world.   As the Global Head of Legal Innovation & Emerging Solutions at Verizon, Gabrielle leads teams that are changing the way they work through international collaboration, GenerativeAI, Platforming, & Tech Solutioneering. Herr team motto: We are Change Agents Building Change Agency.  Gabrielle is also lead lawyer on efforts enabling business transformation from location to workforce readiness. Externally, she is a governing officer of the American Bar Association Antitrust Section, ABA House of Delegates delegate; Commissioner to the ABA Commission on Women in the Profession; & Co-Chair of the ABA Section AI Task Force. Gabrielle co-hosts the Lifting Up podcast, & is a frequent speaker & writer on competition, technology, compliance, business, sustainability, transformation, & diversity issues.   Carly has been with AirBnB for nearly a decade where she counsels & supports Airbnb's global business on risk & regulatory matters (Litigation, Tax, Public Policy, Trust & Law Enforcement Response). Prior to her time at AirBnB, Carly practised as a barrister of law for 13 yeras. Carly practised as a specialist in civil litigation, which involved case preparation & advocacy before the Courts, detailed legal research & preparation of legal pleadings, providing legal advice to solicitors & clients, & predicting & interpreting legal outcomes. Carly is also a board member for the Dublin Fringe Festival.   This invite-only event is designed for senior legal professionals navigating the evolving legal & regulatory landscape. Expect honest insights, meaningful dialogue, & a chance to connect with peers in a relaxed setting.   Space is limited so please RSVP to secure your invitation to join us here.

events.name: Visions Summit
events.description: About EventVISIONS Summit: NYC - Where Culture, Commerce, & the Future Converge Join us at the Museum of Modern Art for a one-day summit exploring the forces shaping the next era of commerce. Centered on this year's theme-We Are Builders of Worlds- the VISIONS Summit will explore mythmaking, design systems, brand legacy, & narrative strategy. The content is crafted to bring a fresh perspective to the retail & eCommerce landscape, inspiring marketing, CX, & innovation professionals to think beyond best practices & engage in meaningful transformation. Programmed by the editorial team at Future Commerce, this half-day event features a curated mix of keynotes, 1-on-1 interviews, & moderated panels with culture-shaping voices across media, creative arts, brand building, & technology. Through immersive storytelling, unexpected conversations, & cultural insight, this event is where the future gets built. This one-day-only experience will be held in the Roy & Niuta Titus Theater at the Museum of Modern Art, & only 200 guests will have the opportunity to attend. Full agenda & speakers can be viewed at futurecommerce.com/visions

events.name: AI in Action - Driving Real Impact w/ AI | #NYTechWeek A Strategy Workshop by Proximity Works
events.description: NY Tech Week | In-Person | Manhattan Ready to move beyond AI experiments & actually make it work for your business? Join Proximity Works for an executive workshop designed for leaders ready to move beyond experimentation & into real-world deployment. This isn't an introduction to AI - it's a hands-on deep dive into implementing AI agents, automation, & GenAI solutions in weeks, not months. Discover how leading companies automated operations, increased output by 10x, & launched AI agents at speed. No buzzwords. Just practical, ROI-focused strategies from the team behind AI deployments at some of the world's biggest companies globally. If you're building the AI roadmap for your company, this is your room. Why Attend? AI Agents in Action: See how companies are using AI agents to automate ops, accelerate workflows, & boost output - at scale. We'll unpack real-world systems that: Reduced manual ops by 80% Increased content output by 10x Went from scope  MVP  scale in weeks, not quarters From Idea to ROI: Learn how to identify high-impact opportunities & move fast - from prototype to production. Cut Through the Noise: Understand what actually works-co-pilots, RAG, model fine-tuning, orchestration-and where to avoid the hype. Battle-Tested Strategies: Walk through real use cases that reduced manual effort by 80% & increased content output 10x. Learn from common pitfalls & uncover additional strategies gleaned from actual deployments. Network Intentionally: Connect with CXOs, founders, & product leaders who are actively investing in AI & innovation. What You'll Walk Away With 1 x AI Strategy Consultation Session with Proximity's AI Experts A clear, customized AI roadmap framework built around measurable ROI Strategic playbooks to launch AI agents across ops, content, CX, & internal workflows Clarity on GenAI, co-pilots, & orchestration tools - & how to fit them into your stack Who Should Attend Founders, CEOs, CTOs, Heads of Product, & Innovation leaders across industries - especially those with $50M-$500M in revenue, mid-to-high digital maturity, & a strong mandate to scale AI with real outcomes. About Proximity Works Born in Silicon Valley, built for real-world scale. events.name: NJ Fast Expo
events.description: Join us for our NJ FAST Expo. More details to come! Startups Pitches Listed Below: Squirro turns meaningless data into A.I.-driven actionable Insights. Venteur is an AI-powered benefits marketplace that empowers individuals to take control of their health care. DeepKeep multimodal AI security safeguards machine learning pipelines, promoting secure, unbiased, real time & trustworthy AI solutions. Penelope is a self-service retirement platform for small & micro businesses. Vaultik is the bank for your belongings that unlocks a new world of post sale opportunities including perks, insurance, & credit services for high net worth individuals. Markets EQ provides GenAI solution providing competitive tonal analysis, previously unavailable awareness. Cranium is an AI security & trust platform that delivers security & trust to the AI revolution. Ledge is a command center for payments built for finance teams operating at scale. Column is an AI-powered insurance management platform designed for today's financial professionals. Clarista is a data analytics platform that provides real-time answers from any enterprise platform, acting as a bridge between technical data & AI. OpenVC provides investible unicorn indexes of the most innovative late-stage venture capital companies.  AQ22 is a loan assessment AI solution built exclusively for credit analysts. Our platform boosts loan assessment efficiency, accuracy, & transparency for creditors, credit unions, & alternative lenders.  Indemn builds machine learning-powered insurance conversations. Users can learn about coverage, purchase insurance, & perform service tasks through natural conversation. FinX Capital Markets builds high-performance systems for investors, analysts, & risk managers of capital markets. Kobalt Labs is an AI-powered platform to reduce errors & increase efficiency in manual compliance work. Atidot empowers life insurance & annuity writers to evaluate opportunities in the in-force of business with big data & predictive analytics. Truthifi is a developer of a wealth management platform designed to offer financial advisory services. #NJFAST #Innovation #NextGenStartups #Networking #GameChangers #NewJersey #Startups #Accelerator

events.name: Leaders In AI - Designing & Building w/ AI
events.description: Join us in New York for a dynamic panel discussion with AI leaders from Meta & Salesforce who are shaping the future of artificial intelligence across enterprise & consumer experiences. Hear From Industry LeadersKatya HomaKatya Homa Senior Product Designer, Gen AI MetaKatya leads design for Generative AI at Meta, shaping intelligent user experiences across Facebook Groups. She's a thought leader in enterprise UX & AI, with a background spanning VR/AR, analytics, & product innovation. Daryl MartisDaryl Martis Director of Product, AI SalesforceDaryl leads product for Salesforce Einstein, driving the strategy & execution of AI-powered enterprise tools. With 10+ years in product & engineering, he brings deep expertise in scaling intelligent systems for business impact. Leaders in AIThought Leadership Event Join us for a dynamic panel discussion with AI leaders as they delve into the transformative impact of artificial intelligence on the future of innovation. 01Scaling AI from Prototype to Real-World Impact Turning promising AI prototypes into scalable, user-focused products requires key operational & technical shifts, from aligning stakeholders to refining use cases & managing infrastructure limits. 02Collaboration as a Cornerstone to AI Success Building AI products takes close collaboration across design, engineering, & data science. This topic explores how shared language, respect, & alignment drive more effective, inclusive systems. 03Evaluating AI Products for Real-World Impact Amid rapid innovation, impactful AI products solve problems, streamline workflows, & fit into users' lives. This discussion focuses on moving beyond novelty to ensure AI solutions are sustainable.

events.name: SaaS Founders & Investors Happy Hour
events.description: NachoNacho - The B2B SaaS & AI + Services Marketplace, ZenDesk for Startups, Deel, Morrison Foerster, & Plug & Play are hosting this exclusive Founder + Investor happy hour event alongside key high-profile brands & VC firms. This networking event will be exclusively for SaaS & AI Founders (minimum $1MLN raised), C-Suites, investors, tech media, & tech leaders (partnership leaders are definitely welcome, too). This event will include sponsored drinks & include a rooftop deck component. This is the premier networking event for New York Tech Week.  At New York Tech Week last year, we had an amazing turnout with hundreds of participating investors & Founders. You can see photos from our New York Tech Week event here (https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7204146057440628738) to get an idea of what it was like. We've also done regular networking events for Founders in the New York City area.  We want everyone to have genuine conversations & build real connections here where founders can share their vision, investors can find the next great startup investment opportunity, & media members can get an inside track of the latest deals & tech trends. Thanks to our sponsors! Deel: Deel is an HR software that allows businesses to easily expand their workforce globally. With Deel, you can start hiring employees & contractors abroad while they take all the liability & handle compliance matters for you. Learn more at https://nachonacho.com/product/deel Zendesk for Startups: Zendesk helps startups build better customer service through interactions powered by advanced AI, enabling founders to scale before growing headcount. The best part? You'll get six months free, & its super easy to set up in no time. Learn more at https://nachonacho.com/product/deal-zendesk. MoFo: 170+ ECVC lawyers across 18 global offices advising 2,000+ startups, VCs, & tech-focused clients. We offer full-service supportfrom formation to financings, M&A, & IPOstailored to market conditions. We deliver strategic, end-to-end counsel to help emerging companies grow & succeed. Learn more at http://mofo.com. Plug & Play: The ultimate innovation platform. Our mission is to build the world's leading innovation platform & make innovation open to anyone, anywhere. We do this by connecting entrepreneurs, corporations, & investors worldwide.Learn more at https://www.plugandplaytechcenter.com. Supermomos:Join 20,000+ other tech, business, & finance professionals in NYC by being a part of the Supermomos community. Supermomos is a tech start-up that facilitates curated in-person communities & brings together like-minded professionals to make friends & exchange ideas. The Knowledge Shop: Thefounder community in New York. We prioritiserelationship building over superficial small talk - by bringing folks together at in-person events, & between times, encouraging conversations on our approval-only WhatsApp group. Learn more at https://www.theknowledge.shop. If you're interested in sponsoring, please reach out to andy@nachonacho.com. This event is a part of #NYTechWeeka week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com. By attending this event, you agree to receive a one-time marketing email or text from NachoNacho & its partners with information related to the event & exclusive offers.

events.name: From Hype To Habit - Crafting AI Products That Stick
events.description: AI is having its moment, with companies racing to ride the wave. But one question looms: beyond the initial hype, what kind of AI products will truly endure? Join us for a dynamic panel discussion & hear firsthand from product & design leaders on what it takes to create AI experiences that are intuitive, trustworthy, & built to last. Panelists Include: Allen Yang - Founder & CEO @ Liminary Andrea Nguyen - Co-founder & Director of Design @ Koi Studios Eileen Wang - Senior Product Manager @ eBay Michelle Hilzinger - Product Lead @ Notion Moderated by Emily Fishbaine - Senior Design Researcher @ Globant, Adjunct Professor @ New York University Whether you're a founder, product owner, designer, or engineer, this event will help you build AI products that not only capture attention, but earn lasting user trust & engagement. ------- This event is hosted by Koi Studios (koistudios.io) & Brooklyn Product Design (bkproductdesign.com). Venue support by BrainStation (brainstation.io). About the hosts: Koi Studios, a digital product design studio, partners with ambitious teams to design powerful, human-centered products that solve real problems & create a kinder tomorrow. Brooklyn Product Design connects Brooklyn & NYC's product design community together through curiosity & shared learning via speaker talks, social events, & hackathons in a safe space. BrainStation works with industry leaders from the most innovative companies, developing cutting-edge digital education that has empowered more than 100,000 professionals & some of the largest corporations in the world. This event is a part of #NYTechWeek-a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

events.name: Techstars Vertical Network Kickoff
events.description: Join Techstars, in partnership with PwC, Brex, Paul Hastings, & Gusto, for our Vertical Network Kickoff at NY Tech Week on Friday, June 6th from 9am-1pm EST. Were bringing together Techstars portfolio companies, investors, experts, & corporate innovation leaders across our AI, Fintech, Healthtech & Sustainability verticals. Expect a morning packed full of insights on emerging trends, discussions on how startups can successfully collaborate with large enterprises, Techstars alumni showcases, & networking. What to Expect:Opening Remarks with Techstars COO, Shirley Romig: Learn more about the power of Techstars Vertical Networks.  Vertical Content Tracks: Choose your adventure & dive into AI, Fintech, Healthtech, or Sustainability, each featuring a mix of roundtables & panels with seasoned industry experts.   Vertical Alumni Showcases: Hear rapid fire pitches from selected Techstars alumni with notable traction in each vertical track.  Networking Hour: All join back together to connect with founders, investors, & corporate innovators in a setting designed to spark meaningful collaborations.  Coffee, tea, breakfast, & afternoon snacks included. This is an exclusive opportunity to engage with the Techstars ecosystem & meet the people shaping the future of innovation. Agenda Overview:9:00am  Check-in & networking10:00am  Vertical-specific events.name: ROI Of Clean Code
events.description: Business CasualDrinks & SnacksSoftware shapes the world - & it shapes your business. As software becomes more critical to business strategy, the systems behind it are under pressure & the long-term cost of unsustainable code is mounting.  ABOUT THE EVENTJoin leaders from JPMorgan Chase, the London Stock Exchange Group, a16z, OWASP, & Software Improvement Group (SIG) for an insight-packed session on how sustainable software is becoming a key driver of business resilience, scale, & innovation. You'll hear from technical, strategic, & policy voices on how to:  Define & measure software sustainability in real terms Align C-suite vision with development execution Shift quality left in the development lifecycle Navigate GenAI's long-term impact on software practices Leverage sustainable systems as a competitive & compliance advantage  WHO IS THIS EVENT FOR?Designed for both engineering & business leaders, this session combines real-world insights with strategic foresight - & features key findings from SIG's upcoming State of Software 2025 report, including industry-specific benchmarks.  AGENDA3:00 PM - Arrival & Welcome 3:30 PM - Opening Remarks & 2025 Industry InsightsLuc Brandts, CEO, SIG 3:40 PM - Keynote | Sustainable Software Engineering: From Intention to ExecutionPepijn Senders, Head of Innovation, SIG 4:10 PM - Panel DiscussionSpeakers from LSEG, JPMorgan Chase, a16z, OWASP, & SIG Engineering, business, & policy perspectives on software sustainability 4:30 PM - Audience Q&A 4:45 PM - Snacks, Drinks & Networking Whether you own the roadmap or the revenue, this conversation will reshape how you think about software's role in driving growth.  For more information, visit www.softwareimprovementgroup.com This event is part of #NYTechWeek - a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

events.name: NYU International Hospitality Investment Forum
events.description: Join 2,200+ hospitality visionaries, innovators, & industry leaders at the leading hospitality investment forum, where our 2025 theme "Engagement Drives Returns" explores how strategic stakeholder engagement drives market results. Key stakeholders will gather to shape the future of hospitality real estate. NYU International Hospitality Industry Investment Conference became NYU International Hospitality Investment Forum:This secures NYU IHIF's place within the global IHIF portfolio, representing the US market as a major player within hospitality investment.Attending an IHIF event provides participants with a global outlook combined with regional expertise & opportunities. IHIF events are known for bringing in cross border perspectives, use cases & strategies that can be adopted & applied within local markets.By participating in NYU IHIF you are still supporting future hospitality leaders. as you are financially contributing to the Jonathan M. Tisch Center of Hospitality to help fund student scholarships at the NYU School of Professional Studies (NYU SPS).DATEJune 1 - 3, 2025 TIMECheck agenda for details LOCATIONNew York Marriott Marquis AUDIENCEPublic REGISTRATIONOpen to industry professionals CONTACTNYU Hospitality Conferencehospitality.conf@nyu.edu Register NowFind out moreSponsorsChair & CommitteeVenue & AccommodationContacts & PressWhat to expect this year?Industry Leaders: Hear Jeff T. Blau (Related Companies) share insights on real estate development innovation & Matthew Luzzetti (Deutsche Bank) discuss macro- economic trends in hospitality real estate.Enhanced Networking: Connect with C-suite executives & major investors in our optimized networking spacesExpert Panels: Gain essential insights through two days of intensive strategy sessionsMaking an ImpactYour participation goes beyond networking by directly supporting future hospitality leaders through our Conference's scholarship program. Thanks to our generous community, we've contributed over $2 million to support talented students at the NYU SPS Tisch Center of Hospitality. Your tax-deductible contribution to the Tisch Center Scholarship Fund creates opportunities for diverse students pursuing careers in hospitality. Key Conference TopicsInvestment & Capital MarketsAsset Management & OperationsDevelopment & InnovationMarket Segments & Brand StrategyTechnology & Future TrendsSustainability & Climate ResilienceWho Will Benefit from Attending the NYU IHIF?Architects & DesignersAttorneysBrokersConsultantsDevelopersFranchisorsInvestment BankersInvestorsLendersLodging executivesManagersOwnersOperatorsProduct & service providers to the lodging industry* The conference is not accredited to certify CLE or CPA credits. We can provide a Letter of Attendance which can be submitted to an agency of your choice. Please check the requirements of your agency prior to the conference & note that we do not verify the number of hours or sessions attended. To receive a Letter of Attendance, please send an email to hospitality.conf@nyu.edu.

events.name: CMO Series Live 2025
events.description: CMO Series Live Venue: SECOND Floor, 849 6th Ave, NYC Date: 5th June 2025 Conference Timings: 09:00am - 4:45pm Evening entertainment: 4:45pm - late Cost: $750 / 595   In 2024, CMO Series Live brought together over 200 fantastic leaders from legal marketing & professional services at a one-day conference, brought to you by Passle. 2025 will see it return on Thursday, June 5th. The conference includes two streams of content, focusing on case-studies of both marketing strategy & marketing technology, making for a thought-provoking day. There will be plenty of time to network & connect with marketing leaders from across the industry as well as the world's best-in-class technology providers. Attendees are CMOs & Business Development Directors in commercial law firms & strategic advisories, looking to engage in rich, insightful discussions on topics that are shaping the future of professional services marketing. There is also the opportunity to connect with the tech partners who can make plans a reality. Space is limited so early booking is highly recommended.   *Photos & video footage taken at the event may be used for marketing purposes.

events.name: NYC Coffee Club - Early-Stage B2B SaaS Founders & Funders
events.description: Calling all early-stage B2B SaaS founders & funders!  I'm Olivia, Partner @ Forum Ventures, & there are few things I like more than coffee & community.  I'm excited to host this coffee meetup & bring together the early-stage ecosystem for Coffee Club - a no-stress way to connect with NYC VCs & founders. Request a spot to join & get access to the location. About the HostsForum Ventures is the leading early-stage fund, accelerator & AI venture studio for B2B SaaS startups. Founded in 2014, we're on a mission to make the B2B SaaS journey easier, more accessible & successful for early-stage founders. From our Studio to Accelerator to Pre-Seed programming, we have the resources & network to help founders navigate the early stages of their founder journey. Forum is investing in 85+ SaaS companies this year. Pitch Us here. Escalon Services provides end-to-end back-office solutions, including accounting, HR, & tax services, designed to support the growth of startups & scaling companies. Our seasoned CFO advisors specialize in financial modeling, strategic planning, & due diligence readiness, helping founders & finance teams navigate fundraising & operational complexities. By managing your back-office needs, we empower you to focus on innovation & growth. B Capital invests globally in extraordinary founders & businesses shaping the future through technology. With more than $8 billion in assets under management & dedicated stage-based funds, the firm focuses on early-stage to late-stage venture growth investments, primarily in the technology, healthcare & climate sectors. Founded in 2015, B Capital has an integrated, global team across nine locations in the U.S. & Asia. The firm's value-add platform, together with the consulting expertise of its strategic partner The Boston Consulting Group, provides entrepreneurs with the tools & resources to scale quickly & efficiently, expand into new markets & build market-leading businesses. events.name: EU Market Pathways For AI, Climate & Emerging Tech
events.description: Join us on June 5th at Impact Hub New York for a full day of expert-led panels unlocking market entry, funding, & growth opportunities in Europe for AI, Climate, & Emerging Tech. Taking place as part of NY Tech Week, this event will bring together leaders from both sides of the Atlantic for a deep dive:  PANEL 1 - Navigating legal & compliance frameworks in the EU (11 AM)PANEL 2 - Understanding the investment landscape across European innovation hubs (1:30 PM)PANEL 3 - Leveraging soft-landing support through trade & economic development agencies (4 PM) In addition to these panel discussions, participants will get networking opportunities & access to resources, investors, & partners in the EU market. Whether youre just starting to consider European expansion or looking for strategies to scale, this event will give you the insights, connections, & resources you need to succeed across the Atlantic. Co-hosted by the Brooklyn Chamber of Commerce, Impact Hub NY Metropolitan Area, Carter De Luca, & Transatlantic Growth. This event is a part of #NYTechWeeka week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

events.name: NYC Tech Mixer
events.description: KICK OFF SUMMER THE RIGHT WAY WITH THE BEST NETWORKING IN NYC!!! As seen in Entrepreneur Magazine. We're NYC's premier tech networking event with one of the largest membership groups in New York & growing every month! Don't miss this opportunity to meet your next big business connection!   Join us for a healthy dose of startup & tech networking in NYC. Meet your next clientFind your next jobStart a conversation with a new cofounderF ind new friends that share common interestsGet some great advice from our founder  JOIN US FROM 6:00 - 9:00PM DRINKS*AMAZING COMPANYTOP COMPANIESTOP TALENT  COMPANIES OFTEN IN ATTENDANCE (... just to name a few) Google  Sony  Electronic Arts  Hulu  The Honest Company  Disney  Apple  Activision  Live Nation  Dollar Shave Club  Facebook  Snapchat  Trunk Club  ZipRecruiter  Experian  DreamWorks  HBO  Edmunds.com  Buzzfeed  NBCUniversal  YouTube  BeachBody  Playstation  Amazon  SpaceX  Bird  Vice Media  Netflix  GumGum  Vayner Media  Activision  Microsoft  Openai   Connect in Tech is the fastest growing networking group in NYC focusing on tech, startups & entrepreneurship. Professional Networking Events, Panel Discussions. The pandemic was tough on us all but now IRL events are back. NYC represents The Cities startup elite, aspiring entrepreneurs, successful startup founders & venture capital in the New York area. Our core goal is to connect people through IRL networking events, online networking events & large summits.   Enjoy NYC's most successful Startup & Tech Mixer. Mingle with highly successful Men & Women in the Startup, TECH & Entrepreneurial space. Enjoy an amazing night with some of the best in the business! Our events are comprised of the most influential professionals in the area. Our purpose is to help our members forge strong, lasting relationships within the Silicon Beach community - combining Fortune 500/1000s with Entrepreneurs & Startups. We creating unique opportunities & connections to help you grow your business & network. cash bar, No Refunds, Substitutions or Exchanges FAQ (Please Read) ***Event Date & location subject to change as per the organizers discretion. If event changes date your ticket will still be valid for the rescheduled event date or new venue. No Refunds, Exchanges or Substitutions***  Will someone from the sponsored companies be there? Companies who sponsor things such as events, baseball teams, billboards etc. aren't always at an event. if someone shows up that works at one of our sponsors, awesome. We do not guarantee someone from our sponsors will be there. If you can't make an event after ticket purchase? We have events every month. Reach out to us & perhaps we can give you free access to our next event. We want to make sure you get value from our events by making connections. For in person events:  Why isn't food & drinks free? Because we are a startup Networking Organization ourselves. A startup that provides networking as a service with entrepreneurs, startup & tech people. We're here to make lasting offline connections not to feed, bathe & cloth you.

events.name: The Junto Club
events.description: In 1727, Benjamin Franklin & a group of friends founded the Junto Club. The members were artists, creatives, & business folk who were passionate about individual improvement & that of society, who met in the evenings to discuss ideas. Welcome to May's The Junto Club. We're bringing together a highly curated group of tech leaders, founders, & change-makers for a cocktail reception-style affair. Join us this evening to meet the most extraordinary & interesting people in NYC. In the past, we've had C-suite executives from Fortune 500 companies, unicorn founders, media personalities, notable creators, & so forth. We carefully read every application (there is a 5,000+ person waitlist) to place you in a relevant, curated group prior to attendance. This is invite-only. Guests who are not approved will not be able to access the venue. Location revealed upon approval. About the host:Andrew Yeung is the Founder, CEO of fibe - a portfolio of events companies including andrew's mixers, The Junto Series & Lumos House. & the OOO Summit. He is also a Partner at Next Wave NYC, a pre-seed/seed found backed by Flybridge Capital. Andrew's hosted 100+ events for 30k+ tech founders & professionals in 8 cities that were featured in Bloomberg, Business Insider, Fast Company, & Fortune Magazine. Business Insider dubbed him the "Gatsby of Silicon Alley." Stay updated with future events: Newsletter: www.andrew.today Instagram: @andrewsmixers Twitter: @andruyeung

events.name: MetaProp Accelerator Demo Day
events.description: Join us in NYC for MetaProp's 2025 Demo Day on June 17th. Be among the first to meet the next generation of PropTech innovators as the six startups in MetaProp's latest Accelerator cohort take the stage. This exclusive event brings together top investors, industry leaders, & select media to hear firsthand from visionary founders tackling the real estate industry's most pressing challenges. June 17, 20259:00 AM - 12:00 PM ESTMidtown Manhattan (RSVP for address) If you have any questions, please get in touch with Evan Petitt at epetitt@metaprop.com. This marquee event has limited space & fills up fast every year. 2025 Cohort BUILDRFI (Miami) - BuildrFi streamlines financial workflows for construction by offering a clear view of project cash flows & financing options, helping to manage material costs & delayed payments. FRAM ENERGY (NYC) - Fram Energy makes solar deployment for multifamily & single-family rentals profitable & effortless through tenant energy billing software. POPWHEELS (NYC) - PopWheels is revolutionizing urban mobility in America with its e-bike battery swap network, starting with the 80,000+ delivery workers in NYC. PROPRISE (SF) - PropRise is an AI-driven commercial real estate deal sourcing platform, transforming how firms discover & evaluate opportunities. ULAMA (NYC) - Ulama is AI-powered code compliance that automates the approval of construction permit applications. WEALTHIE (NYC) - Wealthie builds more wealth for homeowners by investing home equity in diversified assets without loans or debt.

Join us in NYC for MetaProp's 2025 Demo Day on June 17th. Be among the first to meet the next generation of PropTech innovators as the six startups in MetaProp's latest Accelerator cohort take the stage. This exclusive event brings together top investors, industry leaders, & select media to hear firsthand from visionary founders tackling the real estate industry's most pressing challenges. June 17, 20259:00 AM - 12:00 PM ESTMidtown Manhattan (RSVP for address) If you have any questions, please get in touch with Evan Petitt at epetitt@metaprop.com. This marquee event has limited space & fills up fast every year. 2025 Cohort BUILDRFI (Miami) - BuildrFi streamlines financial workflows for construction by offering a clear view of project cash flows & financing options, helping to manage material costs & delayed payments. FRAM ENERGY (NYC) - Fram Energy makes solar deployment for multifamily & single-family rentals profitable & effortless through tenant energy billing software. POPWHEELS (NYC) - PopWheels is revolutionizing urban mobility in America with its e-bike battery swap network, starting with the 80,000+ delivery workers in NYC. PROPRISE (SF) - PropRise is an AI-driven commercial real estate deal sourcing platform, transforming how firms discover & evaluate opportunities. ULAMA (NYC) - Ulama is AI-powered code compliance that automates the approval of construction permit applications. WEALTHIE (NYC) - Wealthie builds more wealth for homeowners by investing home equity in diversified assets without loans or debt.

events.name: Ugly Talk - From $0 To $10k/Month In Ecommerce
events.description: "From $0 to $10K/Month" Founder Panel will be a 30-minute panel discussion followed by a 15 minute audience Q&A. After the panel, we will break into a casual, conversational networking reception. Event Information:Date: Tuesday, May 27th, 2025 Time: 6:00-8:30pm Doors: 6:00p - 6:45p Founder Panel: 6:45p - 7:30p Networking : 7:30p - 8:30p Location: 131 Greene St, New York, NY 10012  What's the Big DealForget growth hacks - let's talk about what actually works in the early days. This raw, real conversation dives into the unscalable moves founders made to earn their first $1K per month. No theory. No funnels. Just the gritty steps that laid the foundation for serious growth.  What You'll LearnThe Early Hustle: How real founders pushed past $0 & what they wish they'd done differently. Customer Chemistry: What it really takes to win your first few loyal customers - & keep them. Manual Moves: Packing tape. DMs. 3AM emails. The unsexy stuff that built empires. Panelists:User Uploaded ImageMarcus Milione | Co-Founder @ Minted New YorkMarcus Milione lives & works in New York City, where he runs two ventures: Minted New York, which makes menswear, performance apparel & jewelry, & Minted Health, a line of endurance-focused nutrition products. The goal is simple: keep learning, iterate honestly & ship work that is useful.  User Uploaded ImageCharlotte Cruze | Co-Founder @ Alice MushroomsCharlotte Cruze is the Co-Founder & COO of Alice Mushrooms, where she has helped build a brand backed by leading investment firms such as L Catterton & cultural icons including Pedro Pascal, Zac Efron, & Kevin Hart. She was recently recognized as one of's Founders to Watch, & Fast Company named Alice one of the Top 50 Most Innovative Companies in Health & Wellness. She has lived in New York City since 2016 after graduating from the University of Virginia.   User Uploaded ImageGabi Lewis | Co-Founder @ Magic SpoonGabi Lewis is the Co-Founder & Co-CEO of Magic Spoon, a company reimagining your favorite childhood breakfast foods with more protein & less sugar. Magic Spoon launched in April 2019 as a direct-to-consumer brand, & is now found in 22,000+ retailer doors nationwide including Target, Whole Foods, Walmart, & Costco. Forbes named Magic Spoon "the future of cereal", & the company has raised $100mm+ from investors including HighPost Capital, Collaborative Fund, Siddhi Capital, & celebrities including Questlove, Amy Schumer, Odell Beckham Jr, Shakira, & Nick Jonas. Alongside Magic Spoon, Gabi serves on the Board of Directors for Momofuku Goods & Four Sigmatic, & is an angel investor in 20+ consumer brands. Originally from Scotland, Gabi studied Economics & Philosophy at Brown University, before moving to New York City to found Exo & then Magic Spoon.   Moderator:User Uploaded ImageBrandon Blum | Managing Partner @ New EditionBrandon is the Managing Partner of New Edition, a fractional Head of Content shop for eCommerce brands, thought-leaders & service providers looking to launch, maintain, grow & monetize their content marketing efforts. Brandon has spent over a decade in Digital Marketing, working at companies such as Atlantic Media, Meredith Corporation & Axios before starting his own ventures. Brandon possesses a blend of experiences spanning entrepreneurship, eCommerce, branding, digital marketing, digital media, content & storytelling.   EVENT HOSTSUser Uploaded ImageNew Edition We take owned media content for eCommerce brands, thought leaders, & service providers to the next level. We function as a fractional addition to your in-house team. We own the creation & distribution efforts behind long-form & short-form video content, graphic design, & overall campaign strategy. Need an extra hand creating streams of valuable, relevant, & consistent content? Let's Chat!   User Uploaded ImageShopify NY Located in Manhattan's vibrant Soho neighborhood-a hub for local entrepreneurs to connect, learn, & grow. Our space hosts a range of on-site event programming, retail activations, & value-added services. By partnering with real, local merchants, we create events that generate word-of-mouth buzz & seamlessly integrate Shopify technology.    Partnering BrandsUser Uploaded ImageSendlane is an email, SMS, & forms platform that delivers best-in-class deliverability & automation tools at a fair, transparent price.   User Uploaded ImageNorthbeam is a marketing intelligence platform that leverages first-party data & machine learning to help e-commerce & direct-to-consumer (DTC) brands optimize their advertising strategies & drive profitable growth.    Supported By:User Uploaded ImageGREY Journal - Media partner spotlighting modern entrepreneurs (Apple News + Google News)    Event VibesGet a taste of past Ugly Talk energy:  Official Website  2024 Video Recap    About Ugly TalkStarted in 2019, Ugly Talk has been transforming business setbacks into breakthrough aha! moments by hosting unfiltered, no-nonsense events where battle-tested entrepreneurs share their raw lessons & insights. With a growing presence in cities like New York, Chicago, San Francisco, & Miami, we've built a vibrant community of thousands of founders & industry leaders making a profound impact on the entrepreneurial landscape.

events.name: European Tech Rooftop Social & Pitch
events.description: n the spirit of NY Tech Week, Swissnex, The UK Trade Organization, the Consulate General of the Kingdom of the Netherlands in New York, Digital Irish, ACE Ventures, & La French Tech NY are joining forces to host an evening of insights & connections that spotlight Europe's unique edge in global tech. The event will bring together a dynamic mix of founders, investors, & ecosystem builders to explore why many of Europe's most ambitious startups are committed to maintaining operations on both sides of the Atlantic.  Through startup pitches & a candid panel conversation, we'll dive into what makes the European tech model distinct, how founders are navigating global fragmentation, & why transatlantic presence remains a strategic advantage in today's shifting innovation landscape.

events.name: Yacht Party
events.description: New York Tech Week Yacht Party  Exclusive Closing Event For the 4th year in a row, the Neo Club is co-hosting the NY Tech Week closing party! This year, we are partnering with Tavern for an exclusive 4-hour afternoon event aboard a private mega yacht in New York Harbor! This gathering brings together a curated mix of top founders, investors, & industry leaders for an afternoon of inspiring talks, curated networking, & exceptional views. Highlights include: A surprise star speaker during an exclusive speaking segment A curated networking session with high-caliber guests Cruise around the Statue of Liberty (weather permitting) 3 full bars with a large selection of drinks A raffle with exciting prizes & surprises! This isn't just a closing party-it's a return to that "Delirious New York" energy we've all been chasing since before the world paused. The boldness, the magic, the unexpected encounters-the kind of night that reminds you why you fell in love with this city in the first place! This event is a part of #NYTechWeek-a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

events.name: Marketplace Startups
events.description: Tackling the Cold Start Problem is the single biggest challenge facing marketplace founders-how do you build both sides (buyers & sellers) at the same time & keep them coming back? Join us for a raw, no-BS conversation about the good, the bad, & the ugly of launching & scaling two-sided platforms.  What You'll Learn: Real Solutions to the Cold Start Problem : Learn actionable ways to jumpstart supply & demand simultaneously in 2025.  Customer Engagement & Retention: Battle-tested strategies to convert early adopters into loyal, long-term users.  Organic Growth Strategies: Proven tactics to transform early traction into a vibrant, self-sustaining community.  Unfiltered Insights & Hard Lessons: Raw stories about the failures that led to success-no sugar-coating.  Who's on the PanelPanelists: TBD - Founder & Investor lineup to be announced soon. Moderator: Conor Lyden, CEO of TrustapFounder & Chief Executive Officer of Trustap, an award winning Irish Fintech startup with a specific focus on enabling users to buy & sell online with complete peace of mind.  Who Else Is Going Early-stage to mid-stage marketplace founders hungry for growthMarketplace investors scouting the next big thing  Product/tech teams tackling two-sided network challenges  Anyone aiming to build trust, growth, & revenue in a marketplace model  Why You Should Attend Tried-and-True Tactics: Hear exactly how seasoned entrepreneurs overcame the cold start & kept their platform thriving.  A Playbook for 2025: Get strategies that work in today's hyper-competitive landscape-user acquisition, monetization, brand building.  Network with Real Movers: Forge meaningful connections with marketplace experts, potential partners, or even future co-founders.  No-Sugar-Coating Allowed: Ugly Talk is about real stories, real mistakes, & real breakthroughs.  Co-Hosted With: Trustap - an end-to-end transaction solution that seamlessly integrates payments, fulfilment & support into your marketplace or ecommerce store.  GREY Journal - Media partner spotlighting modern entrepreneurs (Apple News + Google News)  About Ugly TalkStarted in 2019, Ugly Talk has been turning business breakdowns into breakthroughs through unfiltered events featuring real-world founders. We've built a community across NYC, Chicago, San Francisco, & Miami - creating thousands of real connections, real lessons, & real results. This event is a part of #NYTechWeek-a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.
