events.name: Early Investors Coffee Hour & VC Data Presentation
events.description: Antler & Harmonic are back with their annual NYTW morning mixer for NYC's early stage investors. We'll be featuring a VC data presentation in the middle. 8:30 - 9:20: coffee & "hey good to see yous"9:20 - 9:50: VC data presentation by Harmonic9:50 - 10:30: more coffee & deal flow Serving Joe's Coffee Company cold brews & espressos onsite About Antler VCAntler works with founders through our signature founder residency, adding value immediately, & taking no equity up front. Founders have the opportunity to pitch our Investment Committee to receive a $200k investment for 8%, allowing them to build for 12-18 months rather than attempting to raise before they have real traction. Apply at antler.co/us About HarmonicHarmonic is the startup discovery platform trusted by teams at NEA, Bedrock, Craft (and more) to find breakout investments. Harmonic provides market intelligence on 20M+ startups with tools designed to create better deal flow. Find your next deal on Harmonic  harmonic.ai ** By registering, you acknowledge that photography & videography will be produced at the event, which may be distributed & shared on social media at our discretion (no names will be publicly shown). Please reach out should you have any questions! This event is a part of #NYTechWeek-a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

events.name: Design After Hours
events.description: An evening for New York City designers, creatives, & design leaders to connect with others who care deeply about the craft. Share insights, spark ideas, & engage with the community shaping the future of design & pushing the industry forward. Design After-Hours brings together creative professionals - from UX & product designers to visual & brand leads - for an evening of connection & conversation. Join other innovators to share insights, explore challenges, & be inspired by the craft. What It's About Design After-Hours is an evening dedicated to fostering meaningful connections between design professionals, from UX & product designers to visual & creative leads. It's a space to explore the latest design trends, share challenges, & engage in insightful conversations that inspire & drive the future of design. Who Should Attend UX/UI designers & researchersProduct designers & service designersCreative directors & visual designersDesign leaders in branding, strategy, & innovationInteraction designers & prototyping specialistsDesign engineers & developersProfessionals interested in evolving the design discipline across industries Agenda 6:00pmDoors Open6:05pmWelcome & Opening Remarks6:15pmConversations & Refreshments8:00pmEvent Close What's IncludedGourmet Light Refreshments - Enjoy a curated selection of artisanal bites & small plates, perfect for connecting with colleagues.Expertly Crafted Cocktails - Sip on handcrafted, shaken cocktails made by skilled mixologists, offering a blend of classic favorites & unique creations.Have dietary restrictions? To ensure we can accommodate your dietary needs, please inform us at least 48 hours before the workshop start date.

events.name: Uniting For Impact - Tech & AI For US
events.description: UNITING FOR IMPACT: Tech & AI for Public Health #NYTechWeek   CUNY SPH & KPMG US Mobilizing Startups, Government, Funders, & Industry to Solve America's Growing Public Health Crises with Technology & AI-Driven Solutions   UNITING FOR IMPACT is a networking event during NYC Tech Week co-hosted by KPMG US & Firefly Innovations, the nation's leading public health entrepreneurship platform at the top-ranked CUNY Graduate School of Public Health.   Over cocktails & hors d'oeuvres, this gathering convenes leaders across government, capital, industry, & innovation to confront America's escalating public health crises through technology, AI, & startup-driven solutions.   The event will spotlight the powerful partnership between Firefly Innovations & professional services firm KPMG US - an alliance that is driving record growth in the annual CUNY Public Health Innovation Accelerator program while expanding its reach & national impact.    Together, they are accelerating public health innovation to confront America's growing public health crises with bold, scalable solutions.   Join a community committed to building a healthier, more resilient nation - while advancing economic growth & strengthening the public health workforce through tech-driven innovation.   Learn more at Firefly-Innovations.org   This event is a part of #NYTechWeek - a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem.    Learn more at www.tech-week.com

events.name: Live:The Room Podcast w/ Partiful
events.description: Since its launch in 2022, The Room has hosted flagship events at tech weeks across the country. This year, in partnership with Season 12 sponsors Mercury & Perkins Coie, Madison & Claudia will record a live episode featuring an interview with Shreya Murthy, CEO & co-founder of Partiful. Following the show, attendees will have the chance to connect with other founders & investors during a cocktail reception. About The Room PodcastThe Room Podcast is a top technology podcast founded in 2020 with the mission of opening the door to the room where it happens. Co-hosts Madison McIlwain & Claudia Laurie have have had over 110 conversations across 12 seasons which have included Sridhar Ramaswamy, the visionary behind Neeva & the latest CEO of Snowflake; Cristobal Valenzuela, the driving force of Runway ML; Guillermo Rauch, the mastermind of Next.js & Vercel; & Michelle Zatlyn, co-founder & COO of Cloudflare & more. They were named to the Forbes 30 under 30 list in media & are ranked in the top 10% of technology podcasts. Season 12 Sponsors About MercuryMercury is the fintech ambitious companies use for banking*, credit cards, & software to power all their financial workflows. Mercury began with the belief that all ambitious founders deserve the products to operate at their best. To do this, we started with the one thing every business needs - the bank account. With powerful banking at the center of their financial operations, more than 200K companies gain the precision, control, & speed they need to operate at their best. To learn more, visit mercury.com. *Mercury is a financial technology company, not a bank. Banking services provided by Choice Financial Group, Column, N.A., & Evolve Bank & Trust, Members FDIC. About Perkins CoiePerkins Coie's Emerging Companies & Venture Capital lawyers are trusted advisors to the world's most innovative companies & most visionary investors. We support startup clients from formation to exit with comprehensive legal & business counseling. Our clients have raised more than $15 billion in the private markets over the past three years between the pre-seed & growth stage. Our extensive corporate law experience, combined with sector-specific business & product counseling, ensure our services are tailored to each client's unique needs, investor expectations, & growth trajectories in cutting-edge industries like AI, aerospace & defense tech, fintech, digital health, gaming, energy innovation, digital media, & consumer packaged goods. This event is a part of #NYTechWeek - a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com. The event hosts & sponsors may use the personal information we collect from & about you for business purposes such as sending newsletters, updates, & other communications we think will be of interest to you, including about our services, programming, & events unless you have opted out of such communications.

events.name: A11yNYC - Celebrating 35 Years Of People & Tech
events.description: This event will be online & in-person. The speaker & YouTube live stream starts at 7:30 pm ET. In-person attendees may arrive as early as 7 pm ET. Important note We're gathering in a special location to celebrate 35 years of people & technology. It will be held at Civic Hall at Craig Newmark Auditorium, 2nd Floor, Civic Hall  The Bernard Goldstein Center. 124 East 14th Street, New York, NY 10003. Description We're working on having multiple speakers give lightning talks. Stay tuned! If you'd like to apply to give a lightning talk (5 to 10 minutes), fill out this form. Accessibility The presentation will have human captions [CC], not automatic captions. There will be an ASL interpreter. For Aira Visual Interpreter access for the Blind & Low Vision community, download the Aira Explorer app on the Apple App Store or Google Play. For access in the physical space, the A11yNYC geofence should apply automatically. To access the online webinar for audio description, use the A11yNYC Access Offer to call, or simply inform your Visual Interpreter that you'd like that offer applied when you connect. Please let us know about any other accessibility requirements two weeks before the event. Livestream Link coming soon Provided by Internet Society Accessibility SIG. Location details The Craig Newmark Auditorium is situated on the 2nd floor of Civic Hall. Upon entering the building, please proceed to the elevators, which provide direct access to the 2nd floor. For security purposes, all attendees may be required to check in at the reception desk in the lobby before proceeding. Transportation options Civic Hall is located near several public transportation options:  Subway: 4, 5, 6, L, N, Q, R, W Trains: Union Square Station is approximately 100 feet from the venue. Bus: M14A/D, M1, M2, M3, M55: These bus lines have stops in the vicinity of Union Square, providing easy access to the venue.  Accessibility  Union Square Station is equipped with elevators & is fully accessible. The building entrance at 124 East 14th Street is ADA-compliant, ensuring convenient access for all attendees.  Parking While street parking is limited, there are several parking garages nearby for those who prefer to drive. Ride-sharing services Cabs & ride-sharing services can drop passengers off directly at the building entrance on East 14th Street. Accreditation All A11yNYC meetups are pre-approved for IAAP Continuing Accessibility Education Credits (CAEC). Sponsors Thanks to AKQA, Deque, Evinced, Equal Entry, & Fable for sponsoring. Want to be a sponsor? Contact meryl@equalentry.com

events.description: Join us at NYC ClimateTech.connect to network, learn, & collaborate on innovative solutions for a sustainable future in the Big Apple! Agenda5:30 PM - 9:00 PM NYC ClimateTech.connect 5:30 - 6:00pm Refreshments & networking reception 6:00 - 6:10pm Introductions, Melissa Roth, Partner, Withum 6:10 - 6:15pm Investor Introductions, David Hemenway, Founder, Angel Nest Investor Panel: -Michael Delucia, Investor, (ex Wellington Management) - TBA - TBA - TBA 6:15 - 7:30pm Guest Presenters - Stephen Johnson, CEO, Illinois Clean Fuels - Dana Chermesh-Reshef, Founder & CEO, Incitu - Christopher Wood, Co-Founder, Applied Biorefinery Sciences - Rajan Kumar, Founder & CEO, Ateios Systems 7:30 - 8:00 Networking Presenting Companies Illinois Clean Fuels Illinois Clean Fuels is developing recycling & synthetic fuel plants that convert municipal garbage into jet fuel, paired with carbon capture & storage. This better-than-net-zero process provides a cost-effective route to help solve New York's waste disposal crisis, recover valuable energy & raw materials, & help decarbonize some of the most difficult-to-decarbonize sectors of the world economy. ICF has raised $5 NYC ClimateTech.connect Come join us for an exciting event focused on all things ClimateTech! Network with like-minded individuals, learn from industry experts, & discover the latest innovations in sustainability. The event will be held at Withum, 1411 Broadway, 9th floor, so mark your calendars & get ready to connect with the NYC ClimateTech community. Don't miss out on this opportunity to be a part of the conversation around creating a more sustainable future!

events.name: Expanding Inclusion To Drive Economic  Growth
events.description: Panel & Networking Reception: Tech & Finance's Role in Expanding Inclusion to Drive Economic Growth APCO is organizing a gathering in New York City on June 3! In this panel, representatives from the technology, financial, & public policy sectors will explore the critical role of major technology companies & leading financial institutions in bridging the digital & financial literacy divide, particularly in emerging economies. Confirmed panelists include: Graham Macmillan, President, Visa Foundation Amee Parbhoo, Managing Partner, Accion Venture Lab, Accion Andrew Rasiej, Founder, Civic Hall @ Union Square; Chairman of the Board, NY Tech Alliance Rebekah Tweed, Executive Director, All Tech Is Human The discussion will highlight how these efforts benefit local communities, drive economic growth, & connect more consumers, entrepreneurs, & SMEs to the digital economy. By addressing key initiatives, barriers, & opportunities, we will demonstrate that "doing good" benefits the entire ecosystem by fueling financial growth. Please join APCO for an engaging discussion! **Light bites & drinks will be available.**  ### APCO is a global advisory & advocacy firm helping clients navigate a complex world & create lasting impact. As trusted & strategic advisers, consultants & creators to some of the world's leading corporations, foundations & governments, we discover & foster valuable connections, insights & solutions that drive our clients' missions & objectives forward. This event is a part of #NYTechWeek-a week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

events.name: Shaping The Experiential Economy
events.description: In this era of hyper digitalization, we see a parallel, seemingly paradoxical consumer demand for real-life experiences.  This dichotomy actually makes a lot of sense.  The more our lives move online & our interactions get mediated by machines, the more we miss & crave physical interactions with other humans & the world around us. This is why we are so excited to bring a unique panel of founders building in the experiential economy space to Tech Week to discuss what they are building, current trends, pitfalls & opportunities in the space, & where they see the space going in the next decade.  We will not only discuss trends in direct-to-consumer experiences but also cover how various businesses have excelled in using IRL opportunities for customer activation or employee retention.   We will also discuss what it's like to fundraise in the space.  The night will end with time for networking with others passionate about the IRL Renaissance happening today. PANELISTS: Radha Agrawal: Co-founder & CEO of Daybreaker, the global morning dance, music & wellness movement with a community of half a million people. Radha is also an angel investor in mostly female owned businesses. Justin Bolognino: Pioneer in immersive experiences that fuse technology, design, & human connection. As the founder of META & Unreality, Justin & his team have crafted multi-sensory experiences for Spotify, Google, & Samsung in collaboration with artists like St. Vincent & Skrillex for Coachella & SXSW. Virginia Frischkorn: Founder & CEO of Partytrick, a software platform revolutionizing how experiences are planned, executed, & scaled through smart design & data-driven insights. Prior to Partytrick, Virginia spent 15 years as a luxury events planner & was recognized by Vogue & Martha Stewart Weddings as one of the top 50 planners in the world. If you have any questions for the panelists, please send them in your RSVP & we will try to get to as many as we can at the end of the panel.  Please email shanna@bookkaria.com if you have any questions about the event. Hosted by Karia, a platform that curates & executes elevated guest activities for our partner hotels & resorts.  Offerings include sound bathing, sourdough making, watercolor, mixology, & cacao ceremony under the stars.  Reach out to us inquiry@bookkaria.com. This event is a part of #NYTechWeeka week of events hosted by VCs & startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

events.name: Hike with EA NYC
events.description:
Please submit interest form here: https://airtable.com/shrGg0fiHeZmdB4tP?prefill_Event%20Registered=4482&prefill_Event=Hike+with+EA+NYC&hide_Event%20Registered=true


Grab some fresh air, and (re-)meet some outdoorsy EA NYCers! Join for a laid-back hike in Harriman State Park.

EA NYC Volunteer Organizer Jacob will be leading the hike. Please see below for travel / meal planning and coordination.

The exact timetable details may change, but this will be similar to the final itinerary:
8:40 am: meet Jacob in Port Authority (south building, 40th St & 8th Ave), upstairs at gate 408. You can reach him on FB Messenger or at jacob.eliosoff@gmail.com.
9:10 am: Short Line bus leaves for Tuxedo, NY (also goes to Middletown). You must catch this bus!
10:12 am: arrival at Rt 17 & Liberty Rd, Tuxedo, NY and walk to the trailhead.
10:30 am: the group will (probably) be doing the "Wildcat Mountain Trail" - see the map on AllTrails. 4.2 miles, "easy" difficulty.
https://www.alltrails.com/trail/us/new-york/wildcat-mountain-trail,
1 pm-ish: at some point along the route there will be a break to eat a packed lunch.
2:28 pm: catching the bus back, again from Tuxedo.
3:33 pm: arrival back at Port Authority Bus terminal.

Stuff to bring:
COMFORTABLE SHOES - good traction, ideally some ankle support
LUNCH, SNACKS, WATER - no restaurants/stores in the park, though don't overburden yourself either
A hat?
Bus is $17.85 each way = $35.70. Full-time nonprofit workers/students are eligible for an EA NYC discount, just ask
Tick protection (ie. long pants tucked into your socks and/or repellant). Ticks can be active any time it is above freezing. Also consider closely inspecting yourself when you get home: the risk with ticks comes if you don't notice one for days. 



****

EA NYC:

events.description:
http://effective-altruism.com/ea/1t5/500_million_but_not_a_single_one_more/


500 million people died, but we turned "Smallpox is" into "Smallpox was". This is one of our species' greatest victories over suffering; it's a good time to celebrate and to remember.
.

events.name: Derek Parfit's Birthday!
events.description:
Derek Parfit was perhaps the greatest moral philosopher of the 20th century, in how he shaped the discipline and raised important new questions. 

He gave us the Repugnant Conclusion and the Hitchhiker's Dilemma, and put a plug for giving 10% into On What Matters, his masterpiece. Had he lived long enough to finish another volume, he had plans to discuss existential risk and dive deeper into questions of how we ought to act.

How to celebrate Parfit's life and work? You could discuss population ethics, philosophize about something else, or consider, as a group, ways to make your lives slightly more rational. (Or just eat cake and sing Happy Birthday and engage in idle, friendly conversation; Parfit surely wouldn't object.)

events.name: Tea Conversations : Creative Intention
events.description:
​Hi friends!

​Welcome to Solei, a social teahouse concept that connects people from different walks of life.

​It is your third place, your city’s living room, where we sit down to talk to one another beyond small talk over a shared cup of tea.

​We had a lovely time last time: see here! https://www.instagram.com/p/DIJmgrXOByF/

​—

​What to expect:

​Arrival (~15min): You’ll be sat down in a group as you arrive while the host serves you tea.

​Conversations (~75min): Chat with the group over tea in a semi-structured manner (depending on the group size, we’ll either speak as one group or break down into smaller groups). We’ll also come together as a group to get to know who’s in the room.

​Free flows (~30min): Grab more tea and have amazing conversations.

​—

​This is one of the many pop-up events to come. And I hope you have an amazing experience. If you cannot make this one, subscribe to our calendar or follow us (IG / X) to be in the loop for the next ones!

​Your ticket includes tea refills and snacks. Dress comfortably (you may need to sit on the floor) and bring your lovely self. Try to come on time (or early), if you are running late send a note to the host via Luma!

​Looking forward to meeting you,

​Coco

​

​If you’re curious about why we’re doing this and the story behind this initiative, read this blog post: Slow Spaces for Conversation.

​––

​Your host: Coco @Solei (http://dearsolei.com) - a social teahouse concept for a conversation with people from different walks of life. Building a third place in New York City for the open-minded to have intentional, authentic conversations with strangers from industries, backgrounds, and values different from our own in a setting that is relaxing, non-alcoholic, and extends beyond small talk.

Your host: Soran @telos.haus - telos is a creative workspace and event venue founded in 2024. We offer daytime coworking, private rentals, and curated programming centered on art, music, and design.

events.name: Work in Progress Annual Showcase: Echoes
events.description:
​Work in Progress NYC’s third annual showcase invites attendees to engage with works spanning a variety of mediums – poetry, painting, ceramics, music, dance, video, sculpture, print, and more – that explore resonance, memory, and connection across time and space.

​Food & beverages will be available for purchase.

​Schedule

​Doors Open - 4:30pm
Gallery Start - 5:00pm
Performances - 7:00pm

​
What is Work in Progress?

​Work In Progress NYC is a monthly series where interdisciplinary creatives share projects they’re working on for feedback and dialogue. It’s a safe and conscious space focused on being raw and vulnerable with whatever stage of creation people are at instead of focused on presenting a finished and polished project. Our larger showcases like this one serve as a space to share more polished projects, celebrate each other's work, and give our artists an opportunity to share their art with a wider audience.

​Price and Accessibility commitment

​We want all our events to be accessible to everyone regardless of background or financial status. The pricing shown here is just to help us break even with costs of hosting the event and we don't make any profit off of these. If you have difficulties paying for the event, reach out to us @workinprogressnyc and we can arrange entry passes for you.

events.description:
RSVP https://www.eventbrite.com/e/shrinky-dink-make-along-tickets-1359369283119

Join us for a fun-filled Shrinky Dink Make-Along where we'll create shrinkable plastic masterpieces together!Come join us at NYCResistor for a fun-filled Shrinky Dink Make-Along event! 
Relive your childhood and come make some shrinky dinks with us! With this fun little project, you can make key chains, earrings, charms, and more! We will provide the shrinky dink paper and various drawing materials so you can create your own one of a kind charm. You just need to bring your creativity!
This class is for everyone, all materials provided, but feel free to bring reference drawings or photos if you want!This workshop is lead by Resistor member Ben K.
This is a masks optional event.
As with all NYC Resistor events, this class is 18+ and governed by our Code of Conduct. The Code of Conduct, as well as accessibility information, can be found at www.nycresistor.com/participate/.
Please note that refunds must be requested 24 hours in advance. If you have any questions, please email classes@nycresistor.com.

events.name: NYCResistor Ham Radio Club
events.description:
Vaccination Proof + Mask + RSVP Required
RSVP https://www.eventbrite.com/e/nycresistor-ham-radio-club-tickets-1113096544729

Monthly Amateur Radio club meeting of NYCResistorRadio curious? Join the NYC Resistor Ham Radio Club (call sign N2YCR) for our open and regular meetings, the 4th Wednesday of each month from 8:00 – 9:30 pm at NYC Resistor (87 3rd Avenue, 4th Floor).
The amateur radio hobby has something for everyone, from Morse code to modern digital modes. Bring projects you're working on, gear to fix, problems that puzzle you, outings you'd like to organize, or just yourself to meet & greet your friendly local hams. Drop by the #n2ycr channel in Discord any time to say hi.
You do not need to be a member of Resistor to be a member of the club, nor do you need to be a licensed amateur radio operator (though of course we hope you'll become licensed with our help).
We aim to create an inclusive, encouraging, and diverse environment to bring together Brooklyn's radio hackers, antenna experimenters, shortwave listeners, toroid winders, satellite chasers, and balloon launchers.
Come make some noise in the weird and wonderful medium of radio!This is a masks optional event.
As with all NYC Resistor events, this class is 18+ and governed by our Code of Conduct. The Code of Conduct, as well as accessibility information, can be found at www.nycresistor.com/participate/.
Please note that refunds must be requested 24 hours in advance. If you have any questions, please email classes@nycresistor.com.

events.name: Beginner Crochet Class: Flower Making **MASKS ON**
events.description:
RSVP https://www.eventbrite.com/e/beginner-crochet-class-flower-making-masks-on-tickets-1373594150079

Learn the basics of crochet, by making two kinds of flowers, and leave with your own handmade earrings or key chain. All materials included.This is a masks required class. Please stay home if you are sick to prioritize the needs of the our immune compromised community.

The class:
I will teach you how to crochet at a beginner level in general along with the techniques you will need to create two different flowers - a daisy and a forget-me-not! Once you are done with both, we will move on to adding metals and using supplies to either make a keychain or make a second copy and a pair of earrings!Before starting the flowers, you will choose whether you want to make earrings or a chain or keychain with your flower, and based on that, I'll help you choose a good hook size to use for your design. If you want to bring your own hooks to use, that's great too. We will be using green, yellow, white, and blue yarn that we will provide and your ticket purchase will include.
I'm going to go step by step and will be happy to correct your hands, hook, or yarn techniques in person almost like a yoga instructor as I teach you the fundamentals. We will go through how to chain, single crochet, double crochet, slip stitch, weave in ends, and how I use the techniques for my crochet earring business where I sell similar products! Once we practice and go through these fundamentals, I'll apply the techniques for the flower with you following the pattern.I'll be showing you and walking through what the instructions mean and how it looks in real time! I'll teach you how to finish both flowers along with how to weave in the ends of the yarn like I do for my own earrings. Then we will select which metals you need from the Resistor's supplies and I'll help you use pliers to attach the metals to the flowers and the keychain circle or earring hooks. If you want to make another copy of one of the earrings to have a matching pair, there will also be time for that if you want to stay longer with me to help you through it a second time.

Who is this class for:
This class is good for someone who has never crocheted before, someone starting out, a beginner, or someone with a little experience who wants to learn to make the flowers and get some spring accessories out of a refresher class, or someone who is more experienced than that but still would benefit from a class on how to crochet some flowers and make earrings or a keychain out of them.
Please reach out for a pay-what-you-can/free ticket if you are unable to purchase a ticket due to a financial barrier. We will have a few need-based spots available if you email classes@nycresistor.comThe Instructor:I have been crocheting for many years now, and I am now a small business seller of crochet earrings- on Etsy and on display @ theNonbinarianBike bookstore. I have made hundreds of crochet flowers and designed some of my own for various projects, and love it dearly.Crocheting has been so much better than I could have guessed for my creative self. It got me through grad school and an unpaid internship, and so many trainings without losing my mind. It is one of my favorite ways to stim as a Neurodivergent while working towards a project. I have a background in mental health and a lot of experience teaching/explaining things/webinar presenting/etc., and I love learning and helping folks gain access to creative therapeutic resources like crafting and crochet.
Please sign up early, as we need 5 students enrolled to run the class.
We are a proactively inclusive space, and prioritize creating opportunities for the whole community to engage with our events, across all identities of race, creed, ethnicity, age, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, physical appearance, and ability; please read our Code of Conduct to familiarize yourself with our community's values before attending. We are also an 18+ space, and accessibility information can be found here.

events.name: Machine Embroidery with Ink/Stitch – Hands-On Workshop!
events.description:
RSVP https://www.eventbrite.com/e/machine-embroidery-with-inkstitch-hands-on-workshop-tickets-1374460310789

Learn to create stunning embroidery designs using Inkscape + Ink/Stitch!This workshop is perfect for beginners and hobbyists looking to dive into machine embroidery using Ink/Stitch (InkEmbroidery)—a free and powerful extension for Inkscape. You’ll learn how to design, digitize, and stitch your own patterns with confidence!

What You’ll Learn:
Setting up Ink/Stitch (InkEmbroidery) in Inkscape
Creating and digitizing embroidery designs
Understanding stitch types, paths, and settings
Exporting files for embroidery machines
Hands-on practice with real embroidery stitching!

What to Bring:
A laptop (Windows or Linux preferred)
If you don’t have a laptop, no worries! We have a limited number available for use.
This workshop will be taught be Resistor member Karen.
This is a masks optional event.
As with all NYC Resistor events, this class is 18+ and governed by our Code of Conduct. The Code of Conduct, as well as accessibility information, can be found at www.nycresistor.com/participate/.
Please note that refunds must be requested 24 hours in advance. If you have any questions, please email classes@nycresistor.com.

events.name: Learn to Use a Laser Cutter to Cut and Etch Your Own Designs
events.description:
RSVP https://www.eventbrite.com/e/learn-to-use-a-laser-cutter-to-cut-and-etch-your-own-designs-tickets-1369929027599

Harness the power of an Epilog 60 Watt Laser! In this class you'll learn everything you need.Harness the power of an Epilog 60 Watt Laser! In this class you'll learn everything you need to know to make the ideas in your head become a reality with a laser.
In this three hour class, you will learn:
How to set up a file for cutting on the laser cutter in Inkscape or Adobe Illustrator.Laser cutter safety including materials testing.Laser cutter operation including configuration, cuts and raster etching in common materials like wood, acrylic, cardboard and others.At the end of class, you will go home with:
One laser cut project, either the class default or a simple one of your own design (see below for details)Resistor Laser Certification, enabling you to use our laser on Craft Nights.Please bring the following to class:
A laptop with either Inkscape (free: download here: https://inkscape.org/release/inkscape-1.2.1/) or Adobe Illustrator installed.
A USB jump drive
The following items are optional if you'd like to try cutting a simple custom design. If your file ends up being too complicated, we may have you do the class default project first, just to learn the laser in the time allotted - but we'll definitely help you with your more complex cut at a free Craft Night in future!
An SVG vector file of a simple shape you'd like to cut for your first cut.A piece of 1/8 inch or thinner wood, acrylic or cardboard you'd like to cut. If you can't bring your own material, small pieces will be made available, or larger pieces for purchase on site.Please be on time for the start of class! If you're late you will miss your slot.
This class will be taught by NYC Resistor member Katie. As with all NYC Resistor events, this class is 18+ and governed by our Code of Conduct. The Code of Conduct, as well as accessibility information, can be found at www.nycresistor.com/participate/.
Please note that refunds must be requested 72 hours in advance. If you have any questions, please email classes@nycresistor.com.

events.name: Delete Your Account: How to ditch X and Elon with Cyd
events.description:
RSVP https://www.eventbrite.com/e/delete-your-account-how-to-ditch-x-and-elon-with-cyd-tickets-1344394272459

Twitter was never good. X is worse. We'll show you how to archive your tweets with Cyd, then delete your content from Elon's website.Remember Twitter, aka the Hellsite? It was terrible, but at least it wasn't owned by a dude who likes making "roman salutes." After you divest from your SpaceX stock and sell your Tesla, come on down to NYC Resistor, where we'll show you how to ditch X but keep a personal copy of your favorite posts using Cyd.
Before the workshop: Make sure you can log on to X on your laptop (not your phone, your laptop) and once logged in, request an archive of your tweets using these instructions: https://help.x.com/en/managing-your-account/how-to-download-your-x-archive. Please do this step at least a week before the workshop, as it may take X a few to several days to process the archive request.BONUS: We'll also help you sign up for Mastodon, an X alternative, if you want.
This event is donate what-you-wish.
This is a masks optional event.
As with all NYC Resistor events, this class is 18+ and governed by our Code of Conduct. The Code of Conduct, as well as accessibility information, can be found at www.nycresistor.com/participate/.

events.name: Intro to Soldering Workshop: Make an LED Tile
events.description:
RSVP https://www.eventbrite.com/e/intro-to-soldering-workshop-make-an-led-tile-tickets-1369929639429

Soldering is one of the most important skills you'll need for working with electronics. Come join us for an introductory through-hole solderSoldering is one of the most important skills you'll need for working with electronics. Come join us for an introductory through-hole soldering workshop. Soldering enables you to create sturdy connections between electrical components. In this hands-on workshop, you'll learn how to use a soldering iron safely and effectively, and get plenty of practice with both soldering and de-soldering techniques. We'll be soldering up some Game of Life kits - LED tiles that generate nifty animations. No previous experience is required, making this introductory workshop a great choice for anyone who's curious about getting started with hardware tinkering! All materials will be provided.
OK, awesome, I want to join!
Please be ready to start at 1pm! We find that you will need this time to get through the whole class. Our classes tend to sell out about a week in advance so if you're interested, you may want to sign up early.
This class will be taught by Resistor member Raphael.
As with all NYC Resistor events, this class is 18+ and governed by our Code of Conduct. The Code of Conduct, as well as accessibility information, can be found at www.nycresistor.com/participate/. Please note that refunds must be requested 72 hours in advance. If you have any questions, please email classes@nycresistor.com.

events.name: NYCResistor Ham Radio Club
events.description:
Vaccination Proof + Mask + RSVP Required
RSVP https://www.eventbrite.com/e/nycresistor-ham-radio-club-tickets-1113096554759

Monthly Amateur Radio club meeting of NYCResistorRadio curious? Join the NYC Resistor Ham Radio Club (call sign N2YCR) for our open and regular meetings, the 4th Wednesday of each month from 8:00 – 9:30 pm at NYC Resistor (87 3rd Avenue, 4th Floor).
The amateur radio hobby has something for everyone, from Morse code to modern digital modes. Bring projects you're working on, gear to fix, problems that puzzle you, outings you'd like to organize, or just yourself to meet & greet your friendly local hams. Drop by the #n2ycr channel in Discord any time to say hi.
You do not need to be a member of Resistor to be a member of the club, nor do you need to be a licensed amateur radio operator (though of course we hope you'll become licensed with our help).
We aim to create an inclusive, encouraging, and diverse environment to bring together Brooklyn's radio hackers, antenna experimenters, shortwave listeners, toroid winders, satellite chasers, and balloon launchers.
Come make some noise in the weird and wonderful medium of radio!This is a masks optional event.
As with all NYC Resistor events, this class is 18+ and governed by our Code of Conduct. The Code of Conduct, as well as accessibility information, can be found at www.nycresistor.com/participate/.
Please note that refunds must be requested 24 hours in advance. If you have any questions, please email classes@nycresistor.com.

events.name: Synthesizer Night @ NYC Resistor
events.description:
RSVP https://www.eventbrite.com/e/synthesizer-night-nyc-resistor-tickets-1374984639069

Gathering musicians, enthusiasts, and curious minds to connect, collaborate, and share their passion for synthesis. BYO synth encouraged!Join us at NYC Resistor, a makerspace in Brooklyn for an electrifying Synthesizer Night! Get ready to unleash your creativity, explore sonic landscapes, and connect with fellow synth enthusiasts in an evening dedicated to the mesmerizing world of synthesizers. This is a pay-what-you-wish event (recommended donation $8).
Synthesizer Night at NYC Resistor is a celebration of all things synthesizer. Whether you're a seasoned musician, an avid collector, or simply fascinated by the possibilities of electronic sound, this event offers a unique opportunity to gather with like-minded individuals and revel in the captivating soundscape of synthesizers.
At Synthesizer Night, participants are encouraged to bring their own synthesizers, from vintage analog gems to cutting-edge modular wonders. It's a chance to showcase your personal setup, experiment with new equipment, and dive into the rich sonic tapestry that synthesizers offer.
For those looking to enhance the collaborative experience, consider arriving early to connect your synthesizer to a MIDI/CV clock. This allows for synchronized timing among all instruments, creating a seamless musical fusion during impromptu jam sessions.
In addition to the collaborative jam sessions, the event provides a unique opportunity for a show and tell/play with anything that attendees bring. Share your favorite gear, experiment with different setups, and engage in hands-on exploration of various synthesizers and electronic music equipment.
The event unfolds in a relaxed and welcoming atmosphere, providing a platform for musicians, enthusiasts, and curious minds to connect, collaborate, and share their passion for synthesis. Whether you're a beginner seeking guidance or an expert eager to inspire others, Synthesizer Night is the perfect setting to engage in conversations, exchange knowledge, and foster a vibrant community. 
Throughout the evening, you'll have the opportunity to engage in impromptu jam sessions, where the collective energy of multiple synthesizers intertwines to create awe-inspiring sounds. Experience the joy of collaborating with fellow musicians, experiment with different genres and styles, and let the unique characteristics of each instrument shape your sonic explorations.
Additionally, for those who wish to showcase their skills and perform, there will be dedicated performance slots. Take center stage, captivate the audience, and let your creativity soar as you demonstrate the full potential of your synthesizer setup. To sign up for a performance slot, email classes@nycresistor.com or join our Discord and post to #synth.
Whether you're an ardent synth enthusiast, an aspiring musician, or simply curious about the captivating world of synthesizers, Synthesizer Night at NYC Resistor is an event that will inspire, educate, and leave you in awe of the boundless possibilities that electronic sound offers. Don't miss this opportunity to immerse yourself in an evening of music, collaboration, and innovation. Mark your calendars, pack up your favorite synths, and join us at NYC Resistor for a night you won't soon forget. We can't wait to see you there!
This is a masks optional event.
As with all NYC Resistor events, this class is 18+ and governed by our Code of Conduct. The Code of Conduct, as well as accessibility information, can be found at www.nycresistor.com/participate/.

events.name: NYCResistor Ham Radio Club
events.description:
Vaccination Proof + Mask + RSVP Required
RSVP https://www.eventbrite.com/e/nycresistor-ham-radio-club-tickets-1113096564789

Monthly Amateur Radio club meeting of NYCResistorRadio curious? Join the NYC Resistor Ham Radio Club (call sign N2YCR) for our open and regular meetings, the 4th Wednesday of each month from 8:00 – 9:30 pm at NYC Resistor (87 3rd Avenue, 4th Floor).
The amateur radio hobby has something for everyone, from Morse code to modern digital modes. Bring projects you're working on, gear to fix, problems that puzzle you, outings you'd like to organize, or just yourself to meet & greet your friendly local hams. Drop by the #n2ycr channel in Discord any time to say hi.
You do not need to be a member of Resistor to be a member of the club, nor do you need to be a licensed amateur radio operator (though of course we hope you'll become licensed with our help).
We aim to create an inclusive, encouraging, and diverse environment to bring together Brooklyn's radio hackers, antenna experimenters, shortwave listeners, toroid winders, satellite chasers, and balloon launchers.
Come make some noise in the weird and wonderful medium of radio!This is a masks optional event.
As with all NYC Resistor events, this class is 18+ and governed by our Code of Conduct. The Code of Conduct, as well as accessibility information, can be found at www.nycresistor.com/participate/.
Please note that refunds must be requested 24 hours in advance. If you have any questions, please email classes@nycresistor.com.

events.name: NYC Civic Fair
events.description: ​Looking to get more involved at the local level—but not sure where to start? Join us for a Civic Fair connecting New Yorkers with local organizations tackling the city's biggest challenges—from housing and transportation to elections and streetscapes. Think of it like a career fair, but for community and civic impact. Meet the people doing the work, explore causes you care about, and find the right place to lend your voice, skills, or time.💪​Groups in attendance will include:​Abundance New York​Climate Club Friends​Indivisible BK​Maximum New York​New Kings Democrats​New Liberals​Open New York​Open Plans​Regional Plan Association​Riders Alliance​Sunrise Movement NYC​Transportation Alternatives​and more!​​We're asking for a suggested donation of $10 to help supplement the cost of food, snacks, and drinks.​​Venmo: @Len3689CashApp: $LennyAn​About your hosts​NYC June Primary Bootcamp​Along the lines of fitness bootcamps, or career bootcamps, the NYC June Primary Bootcamp is meant to get New Yorkers in shape to not just vote in the primary, but take one more step in their community and political engagement. And make it social so it's more fun! There are different categories of actions that Bootcamp participants can complete, earn points, and win prizes. Here's the full details for the Bootcamp and how to sign up:https://nycpolitics101.substack.com/p/2025bootcamp.​Locally Sourced NYC​Locally Sourced NYC's mission is to show New Yorkers the power of local politics. How does local politics work? Why does it matter? How can you get involved in a way that feels additive to your lifestyle?Follow along on our IG:https://www.instagram.com/locallysourcednycpol/​​Brought to you in partnership with Fabrik!​​​​Fabrikis a home for communities where everyone has a place to belong. With spaces designed to feel more like your living room than your office, Fabrik’s third spaces are vibrant hubs where people can come together in real life, explore interests, forge meaningful connections, and build a sense of community.

events.name: Mobility for Near-Term Abundance
events.description: ​Even if we fix housing policy tomorrow, with construction timelines, it could take years for new supply to come online, but improving mobility can bring near-term abundance.​This meetup will explorelow-friction, high-leverage transportation improvementsthat don't require huge capital outlays, but can still deliver meaningful benefits while housing reforms take shape.See this post for more details on possible solutions:https://abstraction.substack.com/p/abundance-starts-with-mobilityWhether or not you can come, if you have 2 minutes, this quickpre-event surveywill help shape the discussion.

events.name: NYC's Government & Rent Regulation System: A Maximum New York Mega-Office Hours Experience (with snacks)
events.description: ​Event breakdown:​6:30pm: doors open, arrival​6:45–7:45pm: NYC government overview​7:45–8:00pm: break, snacks​8:00–9:00pm: Rent regulation overview​How does NYC work? There's a mayoral race!​For the first half of this event, I will present an abbreviated version of the content in my Foundations of NYC class. We will map out the government, discuss the law, and review basic political history.​What is rent control??​During the second half, we will specifically discuss how rent regulation works, what it is, and how to think about it (you will be in charge of formulating your own conclusions beyond that). About half of NYC's rental stock is rent regulated, so it's a vital component of any politician's housing platform.​You can come for one, or both, halves; I recommend coming for both, because understanding rent regulation is hard without gov basics. Each will be about 1 hour (including some breaks), and there will be a decent break between the two halves themselves for snacks and socializing.​What should you be able to do after attending?​Recognize the basic pieces of the NYC government​Know basic NYC political history​Ask good questions about rent regulation to candidates for office​Evaluate claims made by people on social media about rent regulation​Recreate good-faith arguments about rent regulation from multiple perspectives​List relevant primary sources and law relevant to rent regulation​​Know before you arrive:This event will be guided by thefour rules of etiquettethat guide all Maximum New York classes, and is generally for people who mostly agree withThe Sensible Seventy. Get ready to venture draft opinions, be curious, and have a good time.​🇺🇸🗽🏗️

It will include:​Presentations of no more than ~10 minutes​Details on the Abundance Resolution that attendees may vote on.​Updates on city and state politics!​Know before you arrive:This event will be guided by thefour rules of etiquettethat guide all Maximum New York classes, and is generally for people who mostly agree withThe Sensible Seventy.

events.description: ​OTWC is an NYC-based writing community for online writers to write, exchange ideas, and get inspired. Our sessions look like this:​​​​​​​​​​​​​1. ​​​Introduce yourselves and talk about what you plan to write2. ​​​Focused writing session3. ​​​Get together in small groups and discuss how the session went​​​​​​​​​​​​​Snacks and drinks will be provided!​​​​​​​​​​​​If this price is too steep for you for any reason, email us by clicking "Contact the Host" on the left and we’ll give you a pay-what-you-can ticket.​​​​​​​​​​​​​Follow us on Twitter and Instagram @otwc_nyc to stay updated on future events and announcements!​​​​​​​​​​​​​Hope to see you there 🥳

events.name: Female Founders Unwind - La Creme de la STEM™
events.description: ​Join us for unwinding in great company during New York Tech Week! Show up as you are, honore where you are at and mingle with fellow female founders.​AGENDA:​9 - 9:30am: check-in and networking​9:30 - 10am: pitches of Founding Members of La Creme de la STEM™​Abby Rakshit, CEO and Founder atSeason​Yasaman Soroori, CEO and co-founder atConsulta​Holly Diamond, CEO and Founder atWork Onward​Saba Ahmed, co-founder at Tiny Dinner​Jennifer Dwork, co-founder atBummed​Elisabeth Bykoff, CEO and founder atBoxsy​Victoire Mandonnaud, CEO and founder ofOn My Way​Kristin Scholten, Stealth​10 - 10:15am: breath and relax withLizzy Palmer, CEO and co-founder ofSync N​10:15am - 11am: more networking​​ABOUT LA CREME DE LA STEM™:​​​La Creme de la STEM™is the only global, private network for early-stage female founders in tech and science, offering a holistic support system that includes exclusive insights from exited entrepreneurs, vetted investors and perks from corporate partners.Applyto join!​ABOUT CITIZENS​Citizens Private Bankprovides holistic, high-touch client service to startups, venture capital, and private equity firms. We are a trusted partner to established venture capital funds & emerging managers, founders, and LP's, and corporates for Citizens Private Bank. We offer tailored financial solutions and expert guidance. With a rich legacy of supporting innovation and entrepreneurship, we specialize in providing comprehensive banking services to meet the unique needs of the innovation economy with an unprecedented customer service and support.​ABOUT SOURMILK​Founded by two best friends who met at Stanford, Elan and Kiki are on a mission to heal America's gut through real whole foods, starting one yogurt at a time! Sourmilk (@getsourmilk) has all the probiotic benefits of a supplement but in the form of a thick and creamy dairy yogurt packed with protein and healthy fats. We are excited for you to try it!

events.name: NYC Tech Week Prayer Walk
events.description: ​NYC Tech Week Prayer Walk​A Event by GenZtea​Join us for a moment of mindfulness before the hustle of NYC Tech Week​Start your NYC Tech Week experience with intention and community. Join fellow tech professionals, entrepreneurs, and innovators for a guided prayer walk along Manhattan's iconic High Line park.​Event Details​Date:Saturday, June 1st, 2025Time:3:00 PM ESTMeeting Point:Shake Shack at Hudson YardsEnd Point:Chelsea Market​What to Expect​Our journey will begin with a group prayer at Hudson Yards, followed by a reflective walk along the elevated High Line park. As we stroll through this urban oasis, we'll have the opportunity to connect, share intentions for the week ahead, and enjoy breathtaking views of the city.​We'll conclude our walk at Chelsea Market with a final prayer of gratitude and intention-setting for NYC Tech Week.​Who This Is For​This event focuses on Christian founders and investors in the tech community, creating space for faith-centered networking and reflection. However, we warmly welcome participants of all backgrounds and beliefs who are interested in starting NYC Tech Week with intentionality and community.​Why Join?​Center yourself before the busy conference schedule​Network with like-minded professionals in a relaxed setting​Experience a unique perspective of NYC​Set meaningful intentions for your Tech Week experience​What to Bring​Comfortable walking shoes​Water bottle​Open mind and heart​This event is open to all, regardless of faith background or tradition. Our focus is on community, mindfulness, and setting positive intentions for the week ahead.​Register now to reserve your spot!​-----------------------------------------------------------------​About the Organizer​GenZteais a community of Gen Z founders, creators,and investors that brings Gen Z not just in the room, but at the table.​Community Partners​Christians in Techwas founded by Andy Choi to create community and support for Christians working in the technology sector. The organization focuses on integrating faith with professional life in the tech industry.​TechWalkwas created by Christine Farrier Rosemin as a platform for meaningful connection in the tech community through guided walks that combine networking with mindfulness and movement.

events.name: DeSciNYC: AI and Government in NYC
events.description: ​AbstractNew York is considering some of the most ambitious AI regulations in the country. Assemblymember Alex Bores, who introduced several of the bills, will explain what’s on the table and take questions in an open Q&A.​-----BioAlex Bores is a New York State Assembly Member for Manhattan's East Side, recognized for proactive AI legislation. A former data scientist at Palantir with a master's in computer science, he left due to ethical concerns over the company's ICE collaboration. Bores introduced several AI bills focused on consumer protections, data transparency, and digital content authenticity. Elected in 2022 as the first Democrat in the legislature with a computer science degree, he collaborates closely with tech companies to create effective AI policies.-----Supported ByStadium ScienceFreedom-----Stay Connected about Future EventsTwitter:@descinyc_Instagram:@descinyc​PS: Apply to be a member of the DeSci NYC group chat:https://forms.gle/AmUtotmCgWgGxWDp6

events.name: DeSciNYC: Genome Editing in Early Human Embryos
events.description: ​Genome editing tools allow the introduction of precise changes in the DNA. For this ability, they became indispensable for basic biological research and hold great promise for future therapeutic applications. The same technologies are being utilized also in early human embryos for better understanding of key developmental regulators and DNA repair mechanisms. In the future, precise correction of specific genes might enable curing devastating diseases early in the development or decreasing the risks of certain diseases. Although continuous research is needed before such an intervention becomes safe, the first gene-edited babies were already born. This talk aims to contribute to the debate about human germline gene editing by putting it into a broader context of human biology, discussing the benefits, current limitations, and possible future alternatives leading to the prevention of genetic diseases.Stepan Jerabek is a postdoctoral research scientist in the Pediatrics Department at Columbia University. He is also an associate scientist at IOCB Prague, an institute of the Czech Academy of Sciences. Stepan’s interest in early development and genome stability brought him to the Egli laboratory, where he uses genome editing tools to study DNA repair in human preimplantation embryos. In 2024, Stepan was the recipient of the Druckenmiller Fellowship Award from the New York Stem Cell Foundation Research Institute. Stepan completed his doctoral training at the Max Planck Institute for Molecular Biomedicine in Germany and was a research fellow at Harvard Medical School, working in the lab of genetics pioneer George Church.🐦Stay Connected about Future Events:Twitter:@descinyc_Instagram:@descinyc​PS: Apply to be a member of the DeSci NYC group chat:https://forms.gle/AmUtotmCgWgGxWDp6

events.name: Startup Friends - Meditative Walk w/ Satori
events.description: ​You survived Tech Week. Panels. Pitches. Pings. Your brain feels like a tab you forgot to close.​If you have to explain what you do one more time, you might explode. So now what?​Zen out or burn out.​The meditative walkis a slow, tech-free walk through Central Park. Phones on airplane mode. No pitches. No pretending to be “on.”​Just actual humans, moving slowly, enjoying nature and breathing like it’s 1996.​Join a crew of ambitious, slightly-fried New Yorkers as we unplug, walk, and remember how to be human again.

events.name: Founder Debate Night By Antler & Satori
events.description: ​Two teams. One motion. Wildly strong opinions - whether you believe them or not.​Here’s how it works:​We’ll throw out a spicy topic. Teams take sides. Each person gets a chance to make their case, throw a little shade (respectfully, of course), and see who can win over the crowd.​The best arguments? Loud applause.The worst ones? Probably still loud applause.​No debate experience needed. Just bring your brain, your sense of humor, and your willingness to argue passionately about things thatmay or may notmatter.
